title: Academy
summary: Main research building (Keep Lv2); research is blocked while it upgrades; push Advancement/Typography research first because it raises Research Speed and snowballs everything.
content: The Academy is the main research building, buildable very early (Keep Lv2). Research CANNOT be started while the Academy is upgrading (menu opens but you cannot begin), so a bot must sequence Academy upgrades vs research to avoid idle research queues; the Skill Books Shop and Duty slot remain usable. Duty Officer unlocks at Lv20 (increases research speed; ideal Sun Wu, Hammurabi, Eulji Mundeok). Research-category unlock milestones left-to-right: Lv17 Recovery; Lv28 Military Advance; Lv33 Defense Advance; Lv35 complete Advancement through Military Advance; Lv40 complete Defense Advance; Lv41 Offensive/Defensive Mastery. Implied priority: push Advancement FIRST because its Typography (max +70%) and Super Typography (max +100%) raise Research Speed, which accelerates all later research (Construction/Adv Construction/Super Construction similarly cut build times -- the snowball). Research-speed buffs: those Typography researches, Intellect Crown monarch gear (equip before starting), an Academy duty officer, ALSO a Military Academy duty officer (once Keep 36), Civilization Treasure Code of Hammurabi, an American subordinate city, Alliance Science, Victory Column, Celtic Demon dragon. Research-Stone sourcing: best is killing many Vikings (rally alliance members' Vikings for volume); stones can also be produced in the Research Factory but that is very expensive and only for emergencies. Skill Books Shop sells Common skill books at Lv1-3 only, Duty skill books Lv1 only. Keep-gated, cost F/L/S/O to +610% speed at Lv50. Rule for the bot: never let the Academy idle -- pick the highest-priority available node (Construction/Typography first).

title: Keep: Upgrade Requirements, etc
summary: The Keep is the master gate of the city; every other building and most features are Keep-level-gated, so Keep level is the top-priority pacing variable for base development.
content: The Keep is the master gate of the whole city: the world-map number is the Keep level (k25, k40...). No building can pass a given level until the Keep reaches it. Inside the Keep you exchange gems for gold (Levy), use City Buff items, assign a Duty Officer (unlocked Keep Lv16), change Culture and Decoration. Raising Keep level increases max generals held, free levies, gold per levy, gathering speed and in-city production speed. Each Keep level requires Walls at (n-1) plus a specific building: Lv4 needs Walls3+Farm3, Lv6 Walls5+Warehouse5, Lv21 Walls20+Sawmill20; some need research (Lv7 Conscription, Lv14 Adv Dispatch, Lv19 Adv Conscription). Building unlocks by Keep level: Lv2 Academy; Lv3 Tavern; Lv6 Archer Tower, Stables, Workshop, Market, Hospital, Quarry, Army Camp, Watchtower; Lv7 Forge; Lv8 Trap Factory; Lv9 Embassy, War Hall, Bunker; Lv11 Prison, Mine, Art Hall, Holy Palace, Subordinate City Portal; Lv12 Ideal Land; Lv21 Pasture and Research Factory; Lv27 Arsenal; Lv28/33 Wisdom Dome/Wonder gates; Lv30 Dragon Cliff, Wonder; Lv35 Victory Column; Lv36 Military Academy; Lv38 Triumphal Arch. Upgrade cost is Food/Lumber only through Lv6, Stone added at Lv7, Ore at Lv12; from mid-game all four are demanded in huge balanced amounts (Ore often the single largest late). Example Construction-Speed buff rises 0% (to ~Lv17) up to +600% by Lv50. Keep Duty Officer boosts Construction speed and Training Capacity (ideal Hera, Ashoka, Zhuge Liang, Menshikov). Because everything is Keep-gated, keep the Keep on Builder 1 nearly continuously; resource fields only need Keep-minimum levels.

title: Construction / City Development Guide
summary: Priority Academy & Rally Spot first; buffs from research/gear/duty officer speed construction; several buildings disable core function while upgrading.
content: Beginner priority: after raising Keep level, upgrade Academy and Rally Spot first — Academy research raises many buffs, Rally Spot increases march size for gathering/combat. Construction speed boosted by: Academy Construction/Adv Construction/Super Construction research plus Typography/Super Typography to speed that research; Strength Crown gear before starting; Duty Officer at the Keep before starting (ideal Menshikov or Zhuge Liang, +20% base skill); Alliance Science; also Civilization Treasure (Mask of Agamemnon), Victory Column, Dragon (Celtic Demon). Buildings that disable core function during their OWN upgrade: Hospital (no healing, SvS revive ok), Shrine (no general revive), Walls (no fire-extinguish/repair until done, then full recovery), Academy & Military Academy (no research), Forge (no crafting, but Dragon->Ares upgrade at Lv30 still works), Stables/Archer Camp/Barracks/Workshop (no troop training), Research Factory (no research-stone production), Bunker (no troop deposit). Consuming Return Event matters most at Keep level 25+.

title: Embassy
summary: Governs alliance-help count, reinforcement capacity and resource-transport; raising it (and Market) increases how much free alliance help you receive -- a direct multiplier on free build/research time.
content: The Embassy shows reinforcements to you/allies and lets you send back or recall them. Raising its level gives: (1) Reinforcement -- higher capacity to receive reinforcing troops; (2) Construction -- higher number of alliance help received and greater help-time shortening; (3) Transporting -- larger transport amount, more transport times, larger received amount, reduced transport-fee rate. So Embassy level directly governs alliance-help count -- valuable for a bot that requests help on every build/research. Buildable at Keep 9 (also needs Market 1); Keep+Market gated (raise Market first). Raising the War Hall requires the Embassy first. All menus stay usable during upgrade. Main milestone: Lv27 Duty Officer (buffs Reinforcing Troop Attack/Defense/HP; ideal Queen Anne or Heraclius). The page gives no numeric help-count values -- only that higher Embassy level raises the number of alliance helps. For base_dev priority: the Embassy is high value because it raises free-help count, which is free time before any speedup; keep it near Keep level.

title: Barracks
summary: Ground-troop training building; level unlocks higher ground tiers (T2@6 ... T17@50) and training capacity/speed; Duty Officer slot at Lv25.
content: The Barracks is the ground-troop training building; higher level unlocks higher ground tiers plus training capacity/speed. Builds Lv1-50, each level requiring the matching Keep level; from Lv26 also Farm (and Shrine at Lv27/30/32/35), Lv40+ also Triumphal Arch 2 plus a Military Academy Ground Formation research. Troop-tier unlocks by level: T2@6, T3@9, T4@11, T5@14, T6@16, T7@19, T8@21, T9@23, T10@25, T11@27, T12@30, T13@32, T14@35, T15@40, T16@45, T17@50 (highest). Blazon at Lv8. Key milestone: Duty Officer at Lv25 (same level as T10; increases Ground Training Speed and Capacity; ideal Abd al-Rahman I, Liu Bei, Jiang Ziya). Cost scales steeply: Lv1 1,000 food/1,000 lumber; Lv25 ~11M/14M/15M/14M; Lv50 ~119B/152B/156B/152B. Speed +0% through Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50. For a bot, treat Barracks level as the gate for both ground-troop tier availability and ground training throughput; note research/generals/sub-cities cut training TIME, not per-troop resource cost. For a count/wall goal, low-tier ground (T1 no stone, T2) is the cheap meat-shield; high tiers (T12-14) are the real combat strength.

title: Archer Camp
summary: Ranged-troop training building; unlocks higher ranged tiers by level and has the Duty Officer slot at Lv21 (earlier than Barracks).
content: The Archer Camp is the ranged-troop training building; higher level unlocks higher ranged tiers plus training capacity/speed. Builds Lv1-50, each level requiring the matching Keep level; from Lv26 also Sawmill (and Archer Tower at Lv27/30/32/35, then every level 36-50, plus Barracks at Lv44-49); Lv40 also a Military Academy Ranged Formation research. Ranged tier unlocks: T2@6, T3@9, T4@11, T5@14, T6@16, T7@19, T8@21, T9@23, T10@25, T11@27, T12@30, T13@32, T14@35, T15@40, T16@45, T17@50. Blazon at Lv8. Key milestone: Duty Officer at Lv21 (notably earlier than Barracks Lv25; unlocks with T8; increases Ranged Training Speed and Capacity; ideal John Hawkwood, Sulla, John I of Portugal). Costs mirror the other training buildings (Lv1 1,000/1,000; Lv25 ~11M/14M/15M/14M; Lv50 ~119B/152B/156B/152B). Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50. For a bot: Archer Camp gates ranged tier and ranged training throughput; Lv21 is the priority milestone for the cheaper early duty-officer buff. Ranged (archers) are the standard main-damage layer in the meta.

title: Stables
summary: Mounted/cavalry training building (Keep 6); unlocks higher mounted tiers by level; Duty Officer slot comes late at Lv33.
content: The Stables is the mounted/cavalry training building; higher level unlocks higher mounted tiers plus training capacity/speed. Requires Keep 6 (Lv1-6 need only Keep 6), then matching Keep level from Lv7 to Keep 50; from Lv26 also Mine (and Tavern at Lv27/30/32/35), plus Archer Camp at Lv44/49; Lv40 also a Military Academy Mounted Formation research and Victory Column 2. Mounted tier unlocks: T2@6, T3@9, T4@11, T5@14, T6@16, T7@19, T8@21, T9@23, T10@25, T11@27, T12@30, T13@32, T14@35, T15@40, T16@45, T17@50. Blazon at Lv8. Key milestone: Duty Officer at Lv33 (the latest of the four training buildings; increases Mounted Training Speed and Capacity; ideal Oleg of Novgorod, John Adams, Stilicho). Costs match the others (Lv1 1,000/1,000; Lv25 ~11M/14M/15M/14M; Lv50 ~119B/152B/156B/152B). Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50. For a bot: Stables gates cavalry tier and mounted training throughput; note the duty-officer slot arrives late (Lv33), so mounted training speed lags unless a general is hot-swapped in.

title: Workshop
summary: Siege-machine training building (Keep 6); early levels Sawmill-gated, later War-Hall-gated; unlocks higher siege tiers by level; Duty Officer at Lv29. Siege have no upkeep and carry the most gathering load.
content: The Workshop is the siege-machine training building; higher level unlocks higher siege tiers plus training capacity/speed. Builds Lv1-50; early levels require Keep 6 plus a matching Sawmill level (Sawmill progression gates early Workshop). From Lv26 requires Quarry (and Trap Factory at Lv27/30/32/35); from Lv36 requires the matching War Hall level, plus Stables at Lv44/49; higher levels also need matching Keep. Siege tier unlocks: T2@6, T3@9, T4@11, T5@14, T6@16, T7@19, T8@21, T9@23, T10@25, T11@27, T12@30, T13@32, T14@35, T15@40, T16@45, T17@50. Blazon at Lv8. Key milestone: Duty Officer at Lv29 (increases Siege Training Speed and Capacity; ideal Gwon Ryul, Mikhail Kutuzov, Roger II). Costs match the others (Lv1 1,000/1,000; Lv25 ~11M/14M/15M/14M; Lv50 ~119B/152B/156B/152B). Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50. For a bot: siege units have NO upkeep and carry the most load, making them ideal gatherers -- so the Workshop is doubly valuable (siege combat tier and gathering fleet). Note early levels are Sawmill-gated and later War-Hall-gated.

title: Army Camp
summary: March/troop-capacity support building (Keep 6); cheap, Keep-only gated, no troop-tier unlocks; gates Rally Spot Lv26+.
content: The captured Army Camp page provides only the upgrade-requirements table and building-guide links -- no in-page text describing its function and no Duty Officer section. By game role it governs march/troop capacity, but the page does not state this. Requires Keep 6 for Lv1-6, then the matching Keep level from Lv7 to Keep 50; unlike training buildings its requirements are Keep-only (no secondary building or Military Academy prerequisites) and it has no Unlock column, so it grants no troop-tier or Blazon unlocks. Costs are lower than training buildings: Lv1 520 food/520 lumber; Lv25 ~5.6M/7.2M/7.4M/7.2M; Lv50 ~14.3B/18.75B/18.7B/18.75B. Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50. A user comment (unverified) reports only 10 Army Camps buildable, possibly scaling with Keep. The Rally Spot from Lv26 requires matching Army Camp levels (Army Camp 26 at Rally Spot 26 ... 50 at 50), so Army Camp level is a prerequisite chain for Rally Spot progression. For a bot, treat Army Camp as a cheap Keep-only support building to keep leveled so it does not block Rally Spot Lv26+.

title: Military Academy
summary: End-game research building (Keep 36) for battle buffs/debuffs, paid in Tactic Scroll + Gold (not Research Stone); its duty officer also speeds ordinary Academy research.
content: The Military Academy hosts research not available at the Academy -- mainly battle-oriented buffs and debuffs -- with higher levels unlocking more. Crucially it is paid with Tactic Scroll and Gold, NOT Research Stone. Buildable at Keep 36, and it gates the Keep in return: Keep cannot reach 37 until the Military Academy is built (Lv1). Each level needs an escalating Keep level (Lv1 Keep 36 ... Lv15 Keep 50; Lv13 also Rally Spot 48), so it is tightly Keep-coupled end-game content. Research cannot start while the building upgrades. Duty Officer available from Lv1, and importantly the Military Academy duty officer increases research speed at BOTH the Military Academy and the ordinary Academy -- so end-game players run two duty officers (one at each) whenever researching. Ideal Military Academy duty generals: Sargon, Justinian; at the Academy ideally Eulji Mundeok or Hammurabi. Cost/priority: total Tactic Scroll + Gold per level rises steeply through Lv5 (Lv5 ~327k scrolls / ~54.6B gold), but Lv6+ are cheaper per level -- guide recommends minimum research at tier 5 then move to tier 6 first. Lv11-15 costs undocumented. Speed-ups come mainly from killing many bosses. Upgrade resource cost (F/L/S/O) is extremely large. Tactic Scroll and Gold are the binding economic inputs here.

title: Research Factory
summary: Production building (Keep 21, Hospital-gated) making Materials, Research Stones and Historic Medals; producing stones is blocked while it upgrades; stones rarely bottleneck until ~Keep 33.
content: The Research Factory produces three outputs: Materials (to craft equipment at Forge and Wonder), Research Stones (for higher Academy research) and Historic Medals (for Council of State position promotions). Higher factory level yields higher-tier materials and more stones. Buildable at Keep 21, and each level additionally requires the matching Hospital level (raise Hospital first) -- double-gated. Producing Research Stones cannot be done while the factory upgrades. Milestones: Lv21 Lv3 materials; Lv32 Duty Officer; Lv36 Lv4 materials; Lv41 Lv5 materials. Materials: up to 8 queued, free but 2+ hours each (gem-finish is expensive -- avoid); make whatever you lack, but near Keep 33 continuously produce Dragon Scale for Achaemenidae equipment. Research Stones: little need until mid-game -- rarely bottleneck research until ~Keep 33 when the rightmost Academy category Military Advance depletes them; producible with gold+gems (100 pcs = 500,000 gold / 8,000 gems; unit cost does not rise with quantity). Increase daily stone-production limit via quests, factory level and a duty officer; increase stone-production speed ONLY via a duty officer (ordinary research-speed buffs like Intellect Crown do not help, since stones are not treated as Research). Historic Medals need general Tokens/Fragments+Gems; newer generals yield far more. Duty Officer (Lv32) raises stone limit and speed (ideal Margaret of Valois, then Stephen the Great). Cost F/L/S/O, enormous late.

title: Arsenal
summary: Troop-tier-upgrade building unlocked at Keep 27 and permanently capped at Level 1; enables converting lower-tier troops to higher tiers.
content: The Arsenal upgrades already-created troops -- it enables troop-tier upgrades (converting lower-tier troops into higher tiers rather than retraining), with a dedicated Troop Upgrade Guide linked. Buildable at Keep 27. Critically, the Arsenal is capped at Level 1 -- no further upgrades exist. Its single Lv1 row costs 58,000,000 Food / 74,000,000 Lumber / 76,000,000 Stone / 74,000,000 Ore, requires Keep 27, example build time ~75d 21:19 at 0% speed, Power 10. Because it has one level, there are no milestone unlocks, no per-level scaling and no duty officer on this page. Its role is narrow but important: it is the gate that makes troop upgrades possible once you reach Keep 27. The page does not state whether the upgrade function is disabled while the Arsenal itself builds, and offers no other economy/buff effects. For a bot, build the Arsenal once at Keep 27 to enable troop upgrades, then it needs no further attention.

title: Hospital
summary: Heals wounded and revives SvS dead; capacity scales with level plus many buffs; Heal is blocked while it upgrades (do not upgrade mid-war); cannot heal siege/souls/deserters/dead generals.
content: The Hospital heals wounded soldiers and revives SvS dead. It CANNOT heal or revive: siege machines (repaired at Trap Factory), souls (revived at Holy Palace), deserters (recalled at Holy Palace) or dead generals (revived at Shrine). Higher level increases wounded capacity. Buildable at Keep 6; the Research Factory requires the Hospital first. For automation: the Heal function CANNOT be used while the Hospital upgrades; Revive (shown only during SvS) CAN be used while upgrading. Milestone: Lv15 Duty Officer (increases Capacity and Healing speed; ideal Blanche, then Pericles/Skanderbeg). Capacity is increased many ways beyond level: Academy research (Hospital Scale, Super/Supreme Hospital Scale, Adv Medical Power) plus Military Academy Military Drill I-III, the duty officer, wall-defense generals at red star 2 (Richard the Lionheart +30% even at star 0), a Dragon of Thebes defender (+25% Lv10, +35% Lv15), Monarch Talent Lv30 Hospital Capacity (+25%), and a temporary +50% during SvS. Keep-gated 1:1 to Lv50. Note: battlefields like Gaugamela/Constantinople have unlimited hospital capacity, so capacity boosts are meaningless there. Bot rule: do not upgrade the Hospital mid-war (healing disabled).

title: Warehouse
summary: Resource-protection building: higher level shields more resources from plunder and from troop upkeep, and raises the storage cap; a Warehouse duty officer plus buffs multiply protected food dramatically.
content: The Warehouse protects resources: it increases protection from enemy looting, protection of food from soldier upkeep (a larger amount held without being auto-consumed as upkeep), and the storage limit for gathered resources and relics. All menus stay usable during upgrade. It is Keep-gated 1:1 (Keep n per Warehouse n) and is a prerequisite for the Pasture (raise Warehouse first). Only milestone: Duty Officer at Lv23 (increases protection from looting and upkeep; ideal Philippa, then Guinevere). Five ways to increase food NOT consumed as upkeep: (1) raise Warehouse level; (2) research Advancement > Adv Silo (max +200%); (3) defense general Shajar al-Durr (+200%); (4) Warehouse duty officer (Junior +20%, Medium +40%, Senior +60%, Special +100%, Super +150%); (5) Lv30 Monarch Talent Troop Upkeep (+100%). These stack multiplicatively on base capacity: Warehouse Lv30 base 25M -> 160M with Shajar 200% + Medium officer 40% + Adv Silo 200% + Talent 100% (540% total); Lv35 base 70M -> 525M with a Super officer and 600% total. For a bot, Warehouse level plus these buffs define the safe food buffer that survives upkeep drain and raids -- critical for large troop counts where upkeep would otherwise consume city food.

title: Walls
summary: City defensive wall, Keep-gated 1:1 and one of the largest single Power contributors (up to 146M at Lv50); captured page has only the upgrade table.
content: The captured Walls page contains only the upgrade-requirements table plus navigation and comments -- no descriptive intro, milestones or feature-unlock list. From the table, the Walls are Keep-gated strictly 1:1 (each level requires the matching Keep level, Lv1 Keep 1 ... Lv50 Keep 50). Costs are F/L/S/O with Stone/Ore entering at higher levels; construction-speed bonuses begin at +30% (Lv18) to +610% (Lv50). Power scales steeply from 21 at Lv1 to 146,000,000 at Lv50 -- the Walls are one of the largest single Power contributors in the base. Their defensive role is implied by name and cross-references (the Watchtower notes scouting reveals a target's Walls HP; a user comment asks about Wall Repair cost and HP) but this page does not state wall HP, repair rules, or any level that unlocks a function. For a bot, treat Walls as a Keep-gated Power/defense building leveled in lockstep with the Keep; specific defense values are not on this page. Whether upgrading disables any wall function is not addressed (unverified; other pages note Wall repair can be disabled mid-upgrade). [VERIFY] wall HP/repair mechanics from the game.

title: Watchtower
summary: Scouting building; each level unlocks more scout-report detail (fully unlocked at Lv41) and gates high-level Forge upgrades (Forge Lv26+).
content: The Watchtower provides detailed scouting reports and warns you when troops march toward your city; ALL scouting features are fully unlocked at Lv41. Scout feature-unlock levels: Lv4 target's F/L/S/O; Lv8 number of incoming troops; Lv11 number of target's subordinate cities; Lv14 number of defending troops; Lv17 Walls HP, reinforcement amount, Archer Tower level, full trap list+levels; Lv19 Truce Agreement Informant; Lv21 type of incoming troops; Lv25 type of defending troops; Lv27 type of reinforcements; Lv30 target troop buff; Lv37 Main City Defense Generals; Lv41 reinforcements buffs. It is tied to the Forge: the Forge cannot reach Lv26+ unless the Watchtower is also leveled (Forge Lv27/30/33 dragon/Ares/Achaemenidae equipment depend on it). Buildable at Keep 6 (also needs Farm 1); Keep+Farm gated. Cost F/L/S/O, speed to +610%, Power ~62M at Lv50. For a bot, Watchtower level determines scout-report richness and gates late Forge upgrades; mostly a PvP-intel building rather than economy.

title: Market
summary: On-city resource income node: free daily resource tax collections, Black Market shop (unlocked Lv11), event-only Auction House, and a Lv24 duty officer that boosts tax yield.
content: The Market provides three economic functions: a Black Market, Auctions, and resource tax collection. Higher Market level makes Black Market products more luxurious and increases both the number of free tax collections and the amount per tax. Buildable at Keep 6; prerequisite for the Embassy (raise Market first). All menus stay available during upgrade. Tax collects resources a set number of times free, after which it costs Gems; the free count resets in 1 day -- a clean daily-collect action for a bot. The Auction House appears only during event periods. The Black Market unlocks at Market Lv11; the Duty Officer at Lv24 (increases Resource Tax Amount; ideal Yolande of Aragon or Michael VIII, which also buff Ground/Mounted troops). Keep-gated 1:1 (Keep n per Market n); cost Food/Lumber (to Lv6), Stone at Lv7, Ore at Lv12; construction-speed example to +610% by Lv50. For an economy bot: collect free tax daily (never pay gems for extra tax), treat the Black Market as the source for VIP items, production speedups and resource boxes, and value the Lv24 tax-yield duty officer. Detailed Black Market and Auction product/price lists live in separate guides.

title: Tavern
summary: General-recruiting building (from Keep 1); level raises the number of visible recruit slots and unlocks the Wheel of Fortune (Lv3) and Great General Chest (Lv5).
content: The Tavern is where you recruit generals; historic (golden) generals can appear here at level-dependent drop rates. Feature unlocks: it exists from Keep 1 (built with the Keep); Lv3 unlocks the Wheel of Fortune; Lv5 unlocks the Great General Chest; visible recruits rise with level -- 4 generals at Lv6, 5 at Lv11, 6 at Lv16 (more slots = more options per refresh). Strictly Keep-gated 1:1 (Lv1 Keep 1 ... Lv50 Keep 50). Cost F/L/S/O (Lv1 free/instant), speed +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50, Power 10 (Lv1) to 62,000,000 (Lv50). Numeric historic-general drop rates live in a linked survey article, not here. For a bot, Tavern level mainly affects recruit-slot count and unlocks the Wheel of Fortune (a free daily source of speedups, stamina, VIP minutes and gathering speedups) and Great General Chest; no function is listed as disabled during upgrade. The Wheel of Fortune is a good daily-collect target.

title: Wonder
summary: Pre-built building upgradable at Keep 30; source of Dragon unlocks and Civilization Equipment/Treasure buffs; leveled via Glory achievements, Badges and Research Stones.
content: The Wonder is pre-built with all menus usable; it can be upgraded once Keep reaches 30. Core roles: (1) unlocking Dragons as its level rises -- Lv1 Norway Ridge, Lv2 Dragon of Thebes, Lv3 Nidhogg (unlocked dragons live in the Dragon Cliff); (2) crafting Civilization Equipment and unlocking/upgrading/equipping Civilization Treasures. Crafting civilization equipment does NOT require raising/unlocking the Wonder but does require Keep 33. When all four refining attributes of a civilization equipment reach Gold, Advanced Refining unlocks. Menus (Glory, Civilization Equipment, Civilization Treasure, Advanced Refining) are all usable during upgrade. Glory achievements grant buffs, decorations, frames and directly upgrade the Wonder: Server War Ares (top-5 SvS rank) -> Wonder Lv1; Monarch's Glory (King/Conqueror twice) -> Lv2; Invincible Hero (Constantinople top-200 season) -> Lv3. Other Glories enable equipment upgrades (Dragon Boots->Ares Boots, Dragon Ring->Ares Ring) or the Ring of Fafnir art buff. Upgrade requirements (Lv1-3) demand Keep 30, Glory achievements, thousands of Badges, and Research Stones. Equipment crafting speed is buffed by Academy Blacksmith research, Agile Crown monarch gear and a Forge duty officer. For a buff-automation bot, the Wonder is the source of dragon unlocks and civilization equipment/treasure buffs.

title: Art Hall
summary: Collection/buff building (Keep 11) giving rewards and buffs for accumulated generals and artworks; gates Keep 28/33 and the Triumphal Arch.
content: The Art Hall gives rewards and buffs as you collect art and generals; you must raise its level to receive the rewards. Buildable at Keep 11. It must be raised first to raise the Keep to 28 or 33, or to raise the Triumphal Arch. Its four menus: General Hall (rewards for collecting generals), Exhibition Hall (rewards for collecting Artwork), Art Treasure (check/upgrade Art-Treasure buffs), Civilization Cards (obtain/exchange/compose cards, view card buffs). Milestones tie rewards to level: Lv11-25 progressively unlock Exhibition Hall rewards (Lv11 Knight Equipment Hall, Lv13 Oil Painting, Lv15 Stringed Instrument, Lv16 Jewelry, up to Lv25 Mask Hall); Lv12 plus Lv27-35 unlock General Hall rewards (through Lv35 Legendary Hero, Invincible Ares). Keep-gated (Keep 11 through early levels then 1:1), speed to +610%, Power ~48M at Lv50. Artwork Fragment Chests fill the Exhibition Halls; each hall's three tiers require 20/60/200 items and pay Gems, Stamina, speedups, Research Stones and Adv City Teleporters (all free once artworks are collected). For a buff/collection bot, Art Hall level unlocks reward tiers from accumulated generals and artworks and gates Art-Treasure buffs and the Mona Lisa gathering buff.

title: Shrine
summary: Revives dead generals and provides the temporary 24h King's Protection buff after Offerings; King's Protection unlocks at Shrine Lv27+ with escalating combat buffs.
content: The captured Shrine page covers two things: the King's Protection buff and the upgrade-requirements table (no intro paragraph; the revive-dead-generals role appears only via cross-references, not stated on this page). King's Protection is a temporary 24-hour buff available once the Shrine reaches a threshold level, activating after a required number of Offerings. Thresholds/buffs/offers: Lv27 Ranged Attack +10% (14 offers); Lv30 +Mounted Defense +10% (14); Lv33 +Mounted&Ground Defense +15% (14); Lv35 Ranged&Siege Attack +Mounted&Ground Defense +20% (14); Lv36 +25% (19); Lv39 +30% (24); Lv42 +35% (29); Lv45 +40% (34); Lv48 +45% (39). Keep-gated 1:1 (built at Keep 1), cost/Power to 31,000,000 at Lv50. Cross-referenced (from Hospital/Prison pages): dead/executed generals are revived at the Shrine -- its other core function. This page does not state whether revive is disabled while upgrading; treat that as unverified. Offering at the Shrine is also a common event-scoring action (Alliance Competition, Revelation of Horus, Dawn of Civilization) and a small daily gem source -- a good daily-collect action for a bot.

title: Bunker
summary: Troop-protection building (Keep 9) that deposits soldiers out of combat for up to 12 hours; deposit is disabled while it upgrades; capacity 100k-800k depending on level+officer.
content: The Bunker protects soldiers by depositing them out of combat for up to 12 hours; higher level and a duty officer let you deposit more. Critically, the deposit function is NOT available during upgrade. Buildable at Keep 9. Duty Officer unlocks at Lv20 (increases Bunker Protecting Capacity; ideal Caterina Sforza or Clovis I). Maximum Protection Capacity by level+officer: base 100,000 at Lv20 to 400,000 at Lv40; officers add Junior +25,000, Medium +50,000, Senior +75,000, Special +100,000, Super +400,000 -- stated max 800,000 (Lv40 + Super officer). Keep-gated 1:1, cost F/L/S/O, speed +50% at Lv8 to +610% at Lv50, Power 10 (Lv1) to 157,000,000 (Lv50) -- a large late-game Power contributor. For a PvP/defense bot, the Bunker is the ghosting/troop-protection building; deposits must be timed around the fact that the deposit menu is disabled whenever the Bunker itself is being upgraded. Not an economy building, but its Power contribution makes it a base_dev consideration.

title: Trap Factory
summary: Makes defensive traps (four families, one per enemy troop type) and repairs siege machines; higher level unlocks higher trap tiers I-X.
content: The Trap Factory manufactures defensive traps that fight in wall defense, and repairs damaged siege machines (siege are repaired here, not at the Hospital). Four trap families each counter a troop type: Trap (anti-Ground), Rock (anti-Ranged), Abatis (anti-Mounted), Fire Arrow (anti-Siege), all 1,500 range. Higher building level unlocks higher trap tiers I-X (Trap I at Lv1, Trap V at Lv22, Trap X at Lv46; Fire Arrow I at Lv4 to X at Lv50). Each trap costs resources and time and adds Power. Buildable at Keep 8 (also needs Sawmill 1); early levels gated by Keep and Sawmill. Milestone: Lv19 Duty Officer (increases Trap Building Speed and Damaged Siege Machine Capacity; ideal Tachibana Dosetsu, Otto the Great, Askia Muhammad I). Building traps Lv6+ (needs Trap Factory Lv26+) is also how you farm Adv Troop March Speedup in the Gather Troops event. Cost F/L/S/O, speed to +610%, Power ~62M at Lv50. For base_dev, Trap Factory level governs both trap tier/strength and siege-repair capacity.

title: Pasture
summary: Levels up Spiritual Beasts, Dragons, and Seals; gated by Keep 21 + Warehouse; gates Council of State tiers alongside Victory Column.
content: Pasture is where Spiritual Beasts, Dragons, and Seals are leveled/refined. Construction possible at Keep Lv21; requires Warehouse raised first. Council of State level-ups require both Pasture and Victory Column raised first. All menus usable during Pasture's own upgrade. Spiritual Beast unlock milestones: Lv6 Thunder Scorpion, Lv11 Bird of Hurricane, Lv16 Lord of Lava, Lv22 Behemoth King. Dragon unlock milestones: Lv26 unlocks Dragon feature plus Celtic Demon (needs 300,000 gems) and Jawzahr (needs Jawzahr Dragon Pattern); Lv30 unlocks Fafnir (needs 1,400 Dragon Crystals); Lv35 unlocks each dragon's 3rd Talent (requires that dragon at Lv10). Council of State gating: Pasture Lv27→CoS Lv2 start requirement, Lv28→CoS Lv3, Lv29→CoS Lv4, Lv30→CoS Lv5, Lv31→CoS Lv6, Lv32→CoS Lv7-9, Lv33→CoS Lv10-12, Lv34→CoS Lv13-15, Lv35→CoS Lv16-45 (i.e. Pasture Lv35 is needed for all remaining Council of State levels).

title: Farm
summary: Food-production field; its own upgrade never costs food (only lumber/stone/ore), strictly Keep-gated, cheap early and enormous late.
content: The captured Farm page is essentially only the upgrade-requirements table plus building-guide navigation; it gives no descriptive text about food production or upkeep. From the table: the Farm is the food field whose own upgrade cost is paid in Lumber/Stone/Ore -- the Food column is blank at every level (it never costs food to upgrade). Stone enters at Lv7, Ore at Lv12; late-game amounts are large and roughly balanced (Ore often largest). Every Farm level is Keep-gated, requiring the matching Keep level (Keep n per Farm n) as its only listed requirement, so Farm level can never exceed current Keep level. The Speed column is a construction-speed example climbing +0% (through Lv17) to +610% at Lv50. No duty-officer slot or milestone unlocks are documented on this page. Whether food output is neutralized by troop upkeep is not addressed here -- that mechanic is covered on the Warehouse page (protected/non-consumed food). Actionable for base_dev: Farm upgrades are cheap early, explode in cost late, never consume food, and are strictly Keep-gated; treat resource fields as low priority, raising them only to the Keep minimum needed to unlock/advance the Keep.

title: Mine
summary: Ore field, the latest-unlocking resource field (Keep 11); upgrade never costs ore; Keep-gated.
content: The captured Mine page provides only the upgrade-requirements table plus navigation, no descriptive text; by type and cost it is the ore field. It becomes buildable at Keep 11 (rows Lv1-11 list Keep 11), then each level requires the matching Keep level (Keep n per Mine n) -- the latest-unlocking of the four basic resource fields and strictly Keep-gated. Its upgrade cost is Food/Lumber/Stone -- the Ore column is blank (the ore field never costs ore). Stone enters at Lv7. Late amounts large and balanced. Construction-speed example +0% (through Lv17) to +610% at Lv50. No duty-officer or milestone section. Comments (not authoritative) indicate a build-count cap that rises with Keep level (a low-Keep alt could build 5 mines, a main 7; exact figures not given). For a bot: Mine is the ore source, first available at Keep 11, Keep-gated so its level cannot exceed the Keep, cheap early and enormous late, consuming food/lumber/stone. No ore upkeep neutralization is described. Priority: raise only to Keep minimum; ore mainly matters as an upgrade cost late-game rather than a training input.

title: Quarry
summary: Stone field, first buildable at Keep 6; upgrade never costs stone; strictly Keep-gated, cheap early and huge late.
content: The captured Quarry page provides only the upgrade-requirements table plus navigation, no descriptive text; by type and cost pattern it is the stone field. It becomes buildable at Keep 6 (rows Lv1-6 list Keep 6), then each level requires the matching Keep level (Keep n per Quarry n) -- Keep-gated, and unlike Farm/Sawmill/Mine it does not exist before Keep 6. Its upgrade cost is Food/Lumber/Ore -- the Stone column is blank (the stone field never costs stone). Ore enters at Lv12; late amounts large and balanced (Ore largest). Construction-speed example +0% through Lv17 to +610% at Lv50 (checkable in Administration on the monarch detail screen). No duty-officer or milestone section. For base_dev: Quarry is the stone source, first available at Keep 6, strictly Keep-gated so its level tracks the Keep, cheap early and extremely expensive late, consuming food/lumber/ore (never stone). No upkeep offset for stone is described (only food upkeep is documented), implying stone production is net-positive. Priority: raise only to the Keep minimum unless stockpiling stone for troop training that needs it (e.g. T2+ ground).

title: Sawmill
summary: Lumber field with a ~12-hour manual-collection cycle; prerequisite for Archer Camp, Workshop, Trap Factory and Keep Lv21; count and output both scale with Keep level.
content: The Sawmill is the lumber field. Increasing its level increases lumber production speed and the temporary storage cap held in the sawmill. Production is not auto-banked: once production starts the temp cap is reached in about 12 hours, so lumber must be collected before then -- tap the wood icon on the sawmill or the arm icon on the Warehouse. This 12-hour cycle is a key automation trigger for a daily-collect loop. The Sawmill is a prerequisite: the Archer Camp, Workshop and Trap Factory cannot upgrade, and Keep Lv21 cannot be reached, unless the Sawmill is leveled first. Upgrade cost is Food/Stone/Ore -- the Lumber column is blank (the lumber field never costs lumber). Stone enters at Lv7, Ore at Lv12; Keep-gated 1:1 (Keep n per Sawmill n). Construction-speed example +0% through Lv17 up to +610% at Lv50. No duty-officer section. Admin note: the number of sawmills buildable (its Building Capacity) rises with Keep level -- about 7 at Keep 1, 10 at Keep 6, 15 at Keep 33 (unclear if higher). So both per-building output and count scale with Keep. Lumber upkeep is not discussed (only food upkeep, on the Warehouse page), so lumber production is effectively net-positive.

title: Troop Type Guide: Usage & Characteristics (Mounted, Ground, Ranged, Siege)
summary: 4 troop types with rock-paper-scissors counters, roles (PvE/PvP/gathering), stat pros/cons, siege range-by-tier, and combat formula/coefficients.
content: 4 troop types: Mounted, Ground, Ranged, Siege. Basic roles: Mounted=PvE monster hunting + PvP vs Ground (highest attack/speed); Ground=PvP vs Ranged + resource gathering (2nd highest Load, high Defense/HP); Ranged=PvP vs Mounted (best Range, weak Speed/Defense/HP); Siege=PvP vs Ranged + resource gathering (highest Load, longest range, weakest Attack/Defense/HP). Rock-paper-scissors at equal tier/buff: Mounted beats Ground, Ground beats Ranged, Ranged beats Mounted; Siege beats Ranged but loses to Mounted/Ground, and is 'extremely' strong (kill rates 10-23x) vs any lower-tier troops. Close-combat types (Mounted, Ground) beat long-range types (Ranged, Siege) if they can close the distance. Siege range increased in a 2020 update by tier: T1-4=1,400, T5-8=1,556, T9-10=1,711, T11-12=1,867, T13-14=2,178 (ranged troop range is 500). Combat formula (per site admin comment): Number Defeated = Attack x Number of Troops x Troop Compatibility Coefficient x (Attack/(Attack+Enemy Defense)) / Enemy HP. Troop compatibility coefficients (attacker:defender): Ground vs Ground/Ranged/Mounted/Siege = 1/1.2/0.7/1.1; Ranged vs same = 0.8/1/1.2/1.1; Mounted vs same = 1.2/0.8/1/0.9; Siege vs same = 0.35/0.4/0.3/0.5. Power (a rough strength estimate) is identical across all 4 troop types at the same tier despite differing Attack/Defense/HP splits. T12 troops are widely believed unusually strong because the game's starting 'City of Throne' troops were coded as T12.

title: Troop Upgrade Guide (Arsenal)
summary: Arsenal (Keep 27) upgrades troops T10->T14 one tier at a time; costs resources+time; upgrading costs more than building new but converts existing troops.
content: The Arsenal building (requires Keep 27) lets players upgrade existing troops one tier at a time: T10->11->12->13->14; troops below T9 cannot be upgraded at all. Requirements: the destination-tier facility (Barracks/Archer Camp/Stables/Workshop for Ground/Ranged/Mounted/Siege respectively) must already be built to the target level; troops must be inside the city (not gathering, hunting, rallying, or ghosting) and not wounded/in hospital; upgrades take time (speed-up-able with gems) and temporarily reduce monarch power by the upgraded troops' value until completion. Upgrading is NOT counted as 'Troop Training' for activity/event score purposes (except if completed on a 'Power Increase' event day). Economically, building a new T(n+1) troop directly always costs less total resources than building T(n) then upgrading it to T(n+1), though the upgrade step alone costs less than building new. Strategic guidance: avoid mass-building troops below T9 (wastes resources/speedups); aim to start serious training at T10 (Keep 25) or ideally T12 (Keep 30); once at Keep 33+, stop trying to fully ghost troops and instead build large defensive numbers, targeting roughly T13: 500k->1M, T12: 1M->1.5M, T11-and-below: 100k->500k per type over time (~2M+ total troops as a baseline to withstand Keep 35 attacks, buff/debuff-dependent). Upgrading Mounted troops preferentially to T12/T13 is recommended early for monster hunting (e.g. needed to beat Witch 3/4, Warlord 3/4) since Mounted is less useful for city defense.

title: Troops Initial Stats List
summary: Base Attack/Defense/HP/Speed/Range/Power/Load/Upkeep per troop type across tiers T1-T17 (T17 added Oct 2025); all stats rise sharply with tier.
content: Base stats (pre-buff) for Ground/Ranged/Mounted/Siege across tiers T1-T17 (T17 added 10/23/2025). T1: Ground atk100/def300/hp600; Ranged atk130/def100/hp250; Mounted atk220/def150/hp400; Siege atk100/def50/hp100. T17 (max): Ground atk5,660/def15,720/hp32,480; Ranged atk6,280/def5,050/hp12,340; Mounted atk10,100/def6,650/hp18,230; Siege atk5,060/def2,390/hp5,060 (~50-56x T1 values). Speed is constant across tiers per type except Siege: Ground350, Ranged100, Mounted displayed as 600 but internal DB value is 300, Siege75(T1-15)/76(T16-17). Range constant except Siege: Ground50, Ranged500, Mounted50; Siege displayed/internal-DB values: T1-4=1400/900, T5-8=1556/1000, T9-10=1711/1100, T11-12=1867/1200, T13-17=2178/1400. Power (same for all troop types per tier) rises from 2 (T1) to 163(T15), 230(T16), 310(T17) -- roughly 155x T1-to-T17. Load ranking Siege>Ground>Ranged=Mounted; T1: Ground10/Ranged8/Mounted8/Siege14 -> T17: Ground70/Ranged60/Mounted60/Siege100. Upkeep (food/time) ranking Mounted>Ground>Ranged=Siege; T1: Ground0.2/Ranged0.16/Mounted0.24/Siege0.2 -> T17: Ground16.5/Ranged15/Mounted19.8/Siege15, roughly an 80x increase in food upkeep from T1 to T17 -- notably less than the ~155x power increase, meaning higher-tier troops give more power per unit of food upkeep despite far higher absolute upkeep.

title: Dead City Power List
summary: Rough monarch-power thresholds by Keep level to identify a 'dead' (abandoned/no-troop) city before attacking, plus gray zones and alive-city comparisons.
content: Reference thresholds (rough) for identifying a dead/abandoned city by Keep level and monarch power, with a 'gray zone' where it's ambiguous: Keep28 dead if <=16M power (gray zone 16M-22M); Keep26 <=10M (gray 10M-14M); Keep24 <=5M (gray 5M-10M); Keep20 <=1.5M (gray 1.5M-2.5M); Keep16 <=500K (gray 500K-2M); Keep12 <=100K (gray 100K-200K). Power correlates with monarch level as well as Keep level, so a low-Keep city with a high monarch level (long playtime, more research/construction) can show deceptively high power while still being effectively dead -- Keep level alone cannot reliably classify power in the gray zone. Reference survey data shows huge variance among 'alive' cities at the same Keep level depending on investment: e.g. Keep35 alive cities ranged from 338M (monarch lvl33) up to 6.9B (monarch lvl35) in the sample.

title: Ideal Land
summary: Buff building (Keep 12) where placing Ornaments and leveling the building grant construction/troop buffs; leveling needs Prosperity Points earned by PLACING ornaments.
content: Ideal Land is explicitly a building used to gain new buffs. It has a garden where you place Ornaments; collecting and placing more Ornaments yields more buffs, and upgrading the building also grants buffs. Available at Keep 12. Note: the Triumphal Arch cannot exceed Lv5 unless Ideal Land is Lv7+. All menus stay usable during upgrade. Building-level buffs (Lv1-10): Construction Speed +2% -> +47%; All Troops HP, Attack, Defense each +5% (Lv2) -> +45% (Lv10). Leveling the building requires Prosperity Points, gained only by PLACING ornaments (not merely owning them); required points scale 500 (Lv2) to 26,000 (Lv10), each level also needing Keep 12 plus escalating resources. Ornament types: Basic (no buffs/points -- skip), Gem-purchasable (points only; placing all = 450 points for 460,000 gems), Limited (both buffs AND points). Limited ornaments come mainly from the Voyage to Civilizations event via Golden Commemorative Coins/Ladders (coins don't expire). RED Champion limited ornaments give large buffs (e.g. Notre-Dame de Paris: March Size +5000, enemy Ground/Mounted HP -10%, attacking Ranged Attack +15%). Garden expandable with gems (32x32 for 50k gems at Lv3; 40x40 for 100k gems at Lv5). For a bot: the Construction Speed and All-Troops buffs make Ideal Land a worthwhile base_dev target where free Limited ornaments are available.

title: Art Treasure Guide (Buff list, Upgrade requirement, etc)
summary: 16 Art Treasures give permanent troop buffs/debuffs, unlocked via Exhibition Hall/General Hall set completion, leveled 1-8 with Scrolls + Artwork Fragments.
content: Art Treasures are permanent buffs unlocked in the Art Hall by completing collection sets in the Exhibition Hall and General Hall; each of the 16 named treasures (Excalibur, Mona Lisa, Great Imperial Crown, Discobolus, Terracotta Army, Tutankhamun's Mask, Ring of Fafnir, Champion Knight Armor, Nike of Samothrace, Celtic Chariot, Golden Eagle Flag, Trojan Horse, Laurel Wreath, Yasakani no Magatama, Iron Crown, Gold Cup of Eternal Stability) can be leveled Lv1-8 for a stronger buff. Example: Excalibur gives Attacking Ground/Ranged/Mounted Troop Attack +5% (Lv1) scaling to +85% (Lv8); Gold Cup of Eternal Stability gives, when attacking, enemy Ranged Troop Defense -10%->-185%, when defending enemy Ground Troop Attack -10%->-185%, plus own Ground Troop Attack +5%->+55%, all scaling Lv1->Lv8. Leveling up a treasure requires a treasure-specific 'Art Treasure Scroll' plus 4 types of 'Artwork Fragment' (also treasure-specific, e.g. Knight's Sword/Leg Armor/Armor/Helmet for Excalibur); material costs per level jump rise from 40-80 units (Lv1->2) to 1,300-4,180 units (Lv7->8). Activation of each treasure requires unlocking specific Exhibition Hall categories (e.g. Excalibur needs Knight Equipment Hall, Stringed Instrument Hall, Jewelry Hall) or General Hall tiers (e.g. Gold Cup of Eternal Stability needs Era of Glory, Tactical Grandmaster, Eternal Renown general-collection tiers).

title: How to get Resources Efficiently (Items, Gathering, Production)
summary: Ranks resource sources: event-boss resource boxes best, then gathering, then production; lists best boss drops, gathering-buff stacks, and warns against opening resource boxes early.
content: Three sources ranked: (A) items/resource boxes from event bosses -- best; (B) gathering resource tiles; (C) production -- least recommended (monster hunting and gathering beat it). All free/F2P. Boss resource-drop ranking by quantity (x4 RSS types): Azazel 5M, Kraken 4M, Stymphalian Bird 3M, Ymir6 2.9M, Ammit 2.5M, Witch6/Warlord6 2.1M; by stamina efficiency Azazel 400K/stamina, Kraken 320K, Ymir6 290K; Cerberus drops 0 resources. Consuming Return Event (free, repeatable): consuming resources returns more -- spend 2.5B each -> 360M+ each; 10B -> 1.6B+ each; consuming speedups also returns resources. Recommended around Keep 25-31. Do NOT open resource boxes early (food consumed by upkeep, lootable if attacked); avoid Store and Black Market (poor value). Gathering-amount buffs stack: 6 Queen Jindeok (+40%, free at tavern for 21M gold), Korean sub-cities (+3-15% each), Champion's Set (4) (+10%), Monarch Talent Lv26 Gathering Boost (+20%), Mona Lisa art buff (+10%). Gathering speed: maximize general Politics, skill book +45%, Advancement research +180%, King's weapons up to +110%, gathering speedup items +50%, Lv12+ tiles 200K/h, alliance hive buff +25%. Monarch Talent Lv22 Resources Critical gives 10% chance to double a box. Production buffs (passive): Advancement research +170%, Shajar al-Durr +100% (free), production speedups +200%, Shrine offerings +100%/2h, monarch gear +50%, alliance science +15%, hive +35%, Monarch Talent Lv6 +50%. For the bot: gathering is the main free automatable lever; open resource boxes just-in-time, not early.

title: How to get Gold efficiently
summary: Free gold sources: battlefields (BoC/BoG), Crazy Eggs, double-drop Goblin/Bayard/Viking kills, World Boss, plus semi-paid gem-to-gold Levy at the Keep with Coinage maxed.
content: Free/F2P gold sources. Battle of Constantinople (BoC): 2M+ win or lose (min Gold chest x5 ~2-15M; max x20 ~8-60M); requires top-20 alliance and being one of 20 chosen participants. Battle of Gaugamela (BoG): 2.4M+ win or lose (min x8 chests, max x25 = 7.5-55M) plus a separate personal-score reward. Levy at the Keep converts Gems to Gold (semi-paid): keep Coinage research maxed (doubles output); at Keep 28 5,000 gems ~13.5M gold, worsening with more levies -- stop by ~15,000 gems (beyond that only ~5M per 5,000 gems); resets daily at server reset. Crazy Eggs: 4.4M+/day (8-hammer Isabella I egg -> 1.2M; activating 8- and 12-hammer eggs each +1.6M). Kill Goblin with a Double-Drop general (Cleopatra/Baibars + King's Ring): up to 4M gold per Lv7 Golden goblin. Kill Knight Bayard with double-drop: 200k-2M each. Kill Vikings: 2.5-3.5M for 50 easy Vikings, up to 400M in a hard rally. Trade Products Event: 2M (50 exchange items per 1M). World Boss: 5 free/day for 250k-1.5M; top-100 single-hit ranking 250k-5M. Activity score: ~1,916K at Keep 28 (proportional to Keep level). Login Reward (10 consecutive days): 6.2M total. For a bot: gold is mainly needed for research/skill books/Levy; farm it free via battlefields, double-drop Goblin/Bayard hunts and Crazy Eggs rather than paying.

title: How to get Gems
summary: Over 300,000 free gems obtainable per half-month; best free sources are Northern Invaders, World Boss, Treasure Hunt, Server War, Crazy Eggs, daily logins and wall server gifts. Gem mines not recommended.
content: Over 300,000 free gems are obtainable in half a month; gem mines are NOT recommended (inefficient, heavy stamina). Ranked free sources: Northern Invaders (Barbarians) Event up to 510,000/event (500M personal score = 255,000; 2B = 510,000). World Boss: up to 300,000/week -- fight 10x/day (6 days/week), up to 5,000/battle (50,000/day); first 5 free, 6th-10th cost 200/400/600/800/1,000 gems (attack only if reward exceeds cost); top-100 damage adds 4,000-10,000. Treasure Hunt (Pyramid): up to 189,000/event; monarch ranking 1st = 300,000. Server War (biweekly): 2,000-84,500 by personal score; top-10 monarch adds 50,000-300,000. Crazy Eggs: ~5,600-75,000/week. Monarch Competition: 3,500+/week. Dwarf's Lucky Apple: 15,000/exchange if lucky. BoC: ~8,000+/week; BoG ~1,000+/week. King's Path Day 2: 100-2,800. Daily logins + 7 weekly Shrine offerings: 2,500/week (spend only the first 25-gem offering daily). Activity score 145 daily: 350-1,000+/week. Undead Invasion ~500+/week. Server Gifts on wall ~700+/week (50 gems at 11:00 and 23:00 server time). Battlefield Shop sells gems for badges (5,000 gems = 110 badges). For a bot, gems fund VIP, Levy-to-gold, and event opens -- so systematically collect the daily/weekly free sources (World Boss, logins, Shrine offering, wall gifts, activity score) and treat events as bulk sources; never spend gems on instant-finish or buying resources.

title: How to get Speedup items efficiently
summary: Ranks free speedup sources: Consuming Return Event by far the largest, then Battle of Constantinople, then boss kills (Cerberus and Pan best); many bosses drop speedups 100%.
content: Free/F2P speedup sources ranked. Battle of Constantinople (BoC): 50-220 hours regardless of win/loss (min ~4 chests ~50h if lose <250 score; max ~18 chests ~220h if win with 1,200+). Consuming Return Event (huge, free, repeatable): completing 2.5B of all 4 resources = 640+ days of speedups; 10B all 4 = 3,100+ days; 35B all 4 = 9,500+ days (cumulative). Recommended Keep 25-35 by tier. Gem-consumption returns during the event also grant speedups (8,000,000 gems -> 1,000 days training + 333 days construction; cumulative up to 2,016 days training / 1,104 days construction). Killing bosses (free) -- best per kill: Legendary Cerberus Lv4 248h, Senior Cerberus Lv3 239h, Pan Lv5 205h, Pan Lv4 184h, Pan Lv3 154h; by stamina efficiency Legendary Cerberus 372 min/stamina, Senior Cerberus 358.5, Pan Lv3 308. Verdict: Cerberus and Pan are by far the best, then Ymir, else Griffin/Witch3/Warlord3/Hydra3/Phoenix. Many bosses drop speedups 100% in their chest (Typhon Lv17 27h, Jormungandr Lv16 24h, Ymir5 36h). Random-drop chests: 3 Royal Thieves -> Royal Chest (8h/24h/3-day/7-day speedups); Viking Rescue Chest -> healing speedups. Cerberus1-4 and low Golem/Warlord/Bayard give 0h. For a bot, the strategic move is to hoard resources and time big construction/training pushes to Consuming Return windows -- the single largest free speedup source by orders of magnitude.

title: How to get Hammer efficiently
summary: Hammers smash Crazy Eggs (60-70+ needed/cycle); best F2P source is farming low-level bosses with a double-drop general, plus gathering Lv6+ resource tiles.
content: Hammers are needed to smash Crazy Eggs (60-70 needed to activate all 4 eggs per cycle, up to 100 if unlucky); normal monsters otherwise don't drop hammers, making them hard to farm at scale. Ranked sources: hunting bosses with a Double-Drop general (e.g. Bivals + King's Ring + Hurricane Bird, ~55% double-drop) gives roughly a 50% hammer drop rate regardless of boss level - a survey found 44 drops from 85 kills (51%) across Lv1-8 bosses (Zombie through Peryton, 2-7 hammers per 10 kills each) - so it's faster to farm low-level bosses at the map edge than to chase high-level ones; Vikings drop hammers less often (~28%, 14 hammers from 50 Easy Vikings). Gathering resource tiles Lv6+ also yields hammers, roughly proportional to tile level - a Lv6 tile gives about a 70% chance per 1-2 hour gather (higher-level tiles have better odds but take 5-12 hours and are harder to find repeatedly). Raise gather speed via a high-Political-stat general, a gathering-speed skill general (e.g. Lucy), Monarch Gear crystals equipped before arrival, and the 'increase all resource gathering speed' city buff item.

title: How to get Medals efficiently
summary: Free medals from the Trade Products Event, Viking kills, and hunting LOW-level normal monsters and bosses; high-level bosses do NOT drop medals.
content: Free/F2P medal sources ranked. Traded Goods (Trade Products) Event: 500 medals -- needs 200 replacement items (earned via quotas for resource production, alliance donation, stamina consumption, troop recovery, alliance rally participation; low difficulty if you play daily; healing wounded troops speeds the quota). Viking Event: 50 easy Vikings for 182 medals (one per kill; Lv1-19 = 3 each, Lv20-49 = 4, Lv50 = 5); with a high double-drop general (Baibars + King's Ring) nearly 300. Hunt low-level normal monsters and bosses: normal monsters always drop medals regardless of level -- farm the map edge with multiple marches. For bosses, medals are in the chest only for low-to-medium level; HIGH-level bosses do NOT drop medals (Lv1 Zombie/Redcap/Centaur = 2, Lv4-6 = 3, Lv7-9 = 4, Lv10 Griffin = 5; Lv11+ N/A). Garuda: Junior 5, Senior 8, Excellent 20. Royal Thief 4. Lava Turtle, Witch, Bayard do NOT drop medals. Paid: Black Market Medal x100 = 7,000 gems, x200 = 12,000 gems. For a bot, medals (used to promote generals) are best farmed by hunting low-level normal monsters/bosses at the map edge with a double-drop general, plus the daily Trade Products quota.

title: How to raise Monarch Level quickly (How to get Monarch EXP)
summary: Monarch EXP caps General level; best source is Shrine Offering with free Tributes (up to 10M/week), plus Hydra kills and resource tiles Lv13+.
content: Monarch EXP raises Monarch level, which caps how far Generals can be leveled. Best F2P method: Shrine Offering - up to 10,000,000 EXP/week possible at Shrine Lv35 with 6+ gem-paid offers/day (cost escalates 25/50/100/100/200/200 gems, resetting daily) plus free 'Tribute' offerings and Monarch Talent 'Offering' for +20% (max at monarch Lv5). Tributes come mainly from Relics (best - collect 25 fragments, break the tile with a hammer for a chance at 10), then Alliance Shop (10,000 points each), then Black Market (300 gems/5 or 500 gems/10), also Wheel of Fortune/Lv2 Candy - roughly 100-180 tributes/week obtainable this way. Other ranked sources: Crazy Eggs event 230,000+ EXP/day (~12-day event); killing Hydra always gives EXP - chest gives 5,000-17,000 (Lv1-5) plus a flat 3,500 EXP per kill regardless of chest; normal monsters Lv31+ give 2,600 EXP each (Lv30 and below not worth farming); Resource Tiles Lv13-15 give 10,000 EXP each, Lv16 gives 20,000; Dwarf's Lucky Apple gives 22,500 EXP if lucky. Battlefield Shop/Black Market EXP purchases are explicitly not recommended (bad value).

title: How to get Skill Book
summary: Common skillbooks best farmed via Relics, Gather Troops event chests, and Viking Challenge; Debuff via Bacchus Tavern gacha; Duty mostly gem-shop/paid only.
content: Common skill books (Military/Development/Subordinate City) ranked F2P sources: Relics - collect 25 fragments for a chance at a Lv4/Lv3 skillbook or a Skill Book Page (best way to get a Lv4 book without paying); Gather Troops event - completing quotas gives a Luxury Treasure Chest per tier, each always containing 1 Skill Book Page (10 pages = 1 Lv4 book), daily players get 10+ chests; Viking Challenge event - Easy 50 Vikings gives Lv3 x1, Normal 50 gives Lv3 x2, Hard 50 gives Lv4 x1 (needs ~100,000 tier-10 cavalry); disassembling unwanted skillbooks gives 1/2/3/4 pages for Lv1/2/3/4 respectively (10 pages = 1 Lv4 book); Wheel of Fortune in Tavern can randomly give a Lv1 book or Skill Book Chest; Academy Skill Books Shop sells only low-level books for gems (Lv1 3,000g, Lv2 5,000g, Lv3 8,000g). Debuff skill books mainly come from the Bacchus Tavern gacha event (collect Bacchus Gold Coins, redeem a Debuff Skill Book Chest for a random Lv1-4) or a $99.99 paid package. Duty skill books: Academy shop sells Lv1 only, limited to a specific building list, for 3,000 gems, or $99.99 paid packages for Lv1-4.

title: How to get Adv City Teleporter a lot
summary: Adv City Teleporter (targeted move) comes free mainly from the Gather Troops event (kill enemy troops) and Crazy Eggs; Store price 2,000 gems -- avoid.
content: The Adv City Teleporter moves your city to chosen coordinates (vs the random plain City Teleporter). Store price 2,000 gems each -- avoid. Focus on free events: Gather Troops and Crazy Egg. Gather Troops Day 5 Destructive Attack (best, up to 32 pcs): kill other players' troops -- 5,000 kills = 1, 10,000 = 2, 20,000 = 3, 50,000 = 10, 100,000 = 15, 300,000 = 22, 500,000 = 32 (NPC sub-city/BoG/BoC kills don't count). Runs ~once or twice a month for ~a week. Efficient tactic: alliance members reinforce an alliance city, then a weak sub-account attacks and loses on purpose so all reinforcers score. Crazy Egg: 4+/day -- smash 3-hammer Lucy egg -> 4 pcs; activate 8-hammer Isabel I egg -> 2 pcs. Less efficient: Daily login + 7 weekly Shrine offerings -> 1/week; Trade Products Event -> 2 pcs; Monarch Competition Stage 5/5 -> 2 pcs; Viking Challenge lucky drop; Wheel of Fortune lucky; Dwarf's Lucky Apple Blessing -> 10 pcs if lucky. Comment: Gather Troops may have been replaced by War Preparation. Also note the Art Hall Mask Hall reward gives Adv City Teleporter x5. For a bot, collect teleporters free from events and the Shrine daily reward rather than buying with gems.

title: How to Get Runestone
summary: Runestones raise General Specialty; best F2P sources are Dwarf's Lucky Apple, Battlefield personal-score rewards, Daily Activity, Champion loyalty, and selling generals.
content: Runestones raise a General's Specialty level. Ranked F2P sources: Dwarf's Lucky Apple - giving a pie to the world-map Witch yields a 'Witch's Gift' with a chance at 100-runestone chests (one player got 300 from 6 gifts); Battlefield personal-score rewards at BoC/BoG/Chalons give 40-350 chests depending on score and win/loss; defeating an enemy player in Battlefield/Server War where both sides lose 30M+ troop power drops runestones; Daily Activity (145 score) gives 5 chests; Champion loyalty level gives escalating chests from Lv1 (3) up to Lv20 (3,000); selling (normal 'Dismiss', not 'Perfectly Dismiss') a trained general refunds runestones - scales steeply with rarity/stars/cultivation, e.g. a Lv38 5-star Gold Historic general with cultivate 500 and Purple x3 Specialty returns 746 chests. Non-free: Auction (40,000 gems minimum bid for 500), Black Market (2,500 gems for 10), paid Advanced Journey event ($4.99 for 1,000) and Event Packages. Chest drop rates by specialty: Leadership 33.85%, Military 30.77%, Glory 24.62%, Immortal 10.77%.

title: How to get VIP Time
summary: VIP TIME (distinct from VIP level) must be active for VIP benefits; it stacks cumulatively; best semi-F2P is a 30-Day VIP from the Black Market for 3,000 gems, plus a free daily VIP-60-min from the wall.
content: VIP time must be active (not just VIP level) to get VIP benefits. Leveling up grants 24h VIP time; after that you must use VIP Time items, which are CUMULATIVE (stacking a new item extends the active period). Recommended method: buy VIP 30 Days on the Black Market for 3,000 gems (semi-F2P -- gems accumulate within 30 days without paying); the Black Market usually shows it at least once per 30-day window. Black Market prices: VIP 30 Minutes 3,000 resources; 60 Minutes 6,000 resources; 1 Day 190 gems; 7 Days 990 gems; 30 Days 3,000 gems. Store (pricier): 1 Day 250 gems; 7 Days 1,500 gems; 30 Days 4,000 gems. The Wheel of Fortune (tavern) can drop VIP 60 Minutes even though not listed. Paid packages can include VIP time. The page recommends always keeping 30-day VIP active. It covers only acquiring VIP-time items, not per-VIP-level benefits (those are on separate linked pages). Note (comment, unverified): a VIP-100-point item appears on the wall server gift at 17:00 server time, and a free VIP 60 Minutes at 19:00. For a bot, keep VIP time continuously active (buy 30-Day on the Black Market with accumulated gems; grab the daily wall VIP items) since VIP unlocks extra queues, faster stamina recovery and buffs.

title: How to raise Rank quickly (how to get Prestige efficiently)
summary: Prestige comes mostly from Shrine offerings (~100K/week) and boss hunting (~150K/week, bigger weight); Lv4+ normal bosses give the most per kill.
content: Prestige determines Rank; raising Rank also requires a high position in the server's Rank Ranking. The guide author earns ~250,000 prestige/week: ~100,000 from Shrine offerings, ~150,000 from boss hunting (boss hunting carries the bigger weight). Offering method: 6+ gem-paid offers/day (25/50/100/100/200/200 gems, resets daily) plus Monarch Talent 'Offering' for +20% (max monarch Lv5), plus saving 20+ free Tributes and spending them all at once weekly (ideally just before the Sunday Rank Ranking reset) - prestige per offer maxes at the 16th offer (+500 each, 1,500 gems). Tributes come from Relics (best/free, 25-fragment tile breaks), Alliance Shop (10,000 points each), Black Market (300 gems/5, 500 gems/10). Boss hunting: kill Lv4+ 'Normal' bosses for 120-500 prestige each (Lv15 Phoenix=500 max); most Event bosses give only 50 (same as a Lv1 Zombie); normal roaming monsters cap at 65 (Lv40 Scythian Knight); Viking event nets 2,260 (Easy)/2,996 (Normal)/3,730 (Hard) prestige for 50 kills, comparable to killing 10 Lv7-10 bosses.

title: Material Chest & Bag Contents
summary: Reference for which crafting materials come from each chest/bag; 12 material types across Lv1-Lv5+; best farmed by hunting many bosses/Vikings.
content: Lists which equipment-crafting materials come from each chest/bag. The 12 material types: Purple Crystal, Blue Stone, Red Agate, Silver Pearl, Meteorolite, Iron, Bronze, Wood, Animal Bone, Leather, Feather, Dragon Scale. Multi-item chests (give one or two): Small Material Chest = Lv1-2 of all 12; Intermediate = Lv2-3; Large = Lv3-4. Single-item colored chests: White = Lv1 (all 12); Green = Lv2; Blue = Lv3; Purple = Lv4. Material Bag (Silk Road) = one Lv5 item of any type. Persia King's Treasure = one Lv5 of Wood/Animal Bone/Leather/Feather/Dragon Scale (organic materials). Pharaoh's Treasure = one Lv5 of Meteorolite/Iron/Bronze/Dragon Scale (metal materials). All contents random within the pool. For efficient farming the page recommends hunting many bosses, especially Vikings. For a bot, materials feed the Forge/Wonder for equipment; farm them via boss/Viking hunts and Research Factory production (Lv3-5 by factory level).

title: Alliance Shop Item List
summary: Spend alliance points (from killing monsters) here; only Tribute (and Truce Agreements) are worth buying at full price -- everything else only during random discount windows.
content: Spend alliance points (earned killing monsters) at the Alliance Shop. Two groups: paid Monthly Card Items ($19.99/month Alliance Monthly Card) and free Normal Items. Spending advice: only Tribute is broadly recommended; Normal Items are worth buying only during limited discount windows (random 24h, triggered by tavern activity), EXCEPT Tribute and Truce Agreements whose prices never change (buy anytime). Key prices (normal/discount, alliance points): Tribute 10,000 (fixed); VIP 100 Points 15,000 (fixed); 10/50/100 Stamina 30,000/50,000/100,000 (discount only); 12h Speed Up 50,000/35,000; 3-Day Speed Up 180,000/126,000; Adv City Teleporter 80,000/64,000; Adv Troop March Speedup 50,000/35,000; Arrest Warrant 50,000/35,000; 8h Gathering Speedup 20,000/10,000; 24h Gathering Speedup 45,000/22,500; 1M Gold 160,000/104,000. Weekly Sale: 8h/24h/3-Day Truce Agreements 30,000/50,000/100,000 (fixed). Monthly Card random items (paid): Blood of Ares, 5K Gems, Medals, Historic General Fragments, Tactic Scrolls. For a bot, buy Tribute and Truce Agreements freely, and grab Stamina/speedups/teleporters only when discounted; alliance points are best held for those discount windows.

title: Black Market Item List
summary: Rotating gem shop, level-gated; best buys are 24h Speedups and Tribute (resource-cost), worst are Stamina and Monarch/General EXP.
content: Black Market stock rotates and refreshes; some items are gated behind market building level. Key F2P-relevant buys (gem-cost unless noted): 24-Hour Resource Production Speedup x1/x2/x5 cost only resources (recommended always-buy), x20 costs 3,800 gems; Tribute x1=30,000 resources, x5=300 gems, x10=500 gems (always recommended - best monarch-EXP value in the game); RSS packages: gold-buyable up to 5M for 1,763,000 gold, or gem-buyable at best rate 360 gems/M (500K or 500M tiers) - market level 30+ reportedly removes the gold-purchase option for RSS per user reports. VIP Time: 30 Days for 3,000 gems is the best VIP value overall. Not recommended: Stamina (50 for 300 gems - the store's 200 gems/unit up to 10 is cheaper; Shopping Spree event beats both at 800 gems/5), and Monarch/General EXP purchases (worse value than farming). Other notable stock: Medal x100/x200 (7,000/12,000 gems), Refining Stone x10/x50 (1,500/7,500 gems), Runestone Chest x10 (2,500 gems), Dragon Crystal x10 (3,500 gems), Scroll chests (6,075-54,000 gems), Gear Lv3/Lv4 (cheaper than store except Lv3 Intellect/Strength Crown), and gold-cost Equipment tiers Gold1/Gold2/Orange/Purple (120,000/28,200/10,400/3,700 gems) - dragon equipment never appears here.

title: How to increase March Size
summary: Levers that raise march size: Rally Spot level, research, VIP, rank, monarch gear, generals, equipment, items.
content: March size = Rally Spot base value (Lv1 800 -> Lv45 860,000) plus % buffs from other sources, all applied to that base; Free/no-spend levers: Academy research Coordination/Adv Coordination/Super/Supreme Coordination (up to +100,000 and +100%), Military Academy Siege/Melee/Charge Formation research; Rank via prestige (Knight +5,000 up to Regent +100,000); VIP4+ gives +4,000 (VIP4) scaling to +160,000 (VIP25), but requires VIP level 4+ AND an active VIP time item; General march-size skillbook Lv1-4 (+3% to +12%); generals with innate march size skill (King Arthur +15%, Genghis Khan +15%, Barbarossa +10% (Lv4 general skill only), Li Shimin +10%, others); +10-14% more from raising a general's star level to 8, +6-16% from specialty; Duty Officer assigned to Rally Spot (requires Rally Spot Lv35) up to +30%/+70,000, or Toyotomi Hideyoshi/Zhang Liang up to +21-22%; Civilization Treasure 'Imperial Seal' unlock/upgrade (Lv1 +3% up to Lv15 +74.5%); Ideal Land ornaments (+250 to +5,000 each, don't need to be placed); Citizen assigned to Rally Spot, star-leveled (Gold/Purple only), up to +48,000; Guerrilla Tactics grade breakthrough at Rally Spot (+30,000 to +160,000, ground marches only); Paid/time-limited: March Size Increase items (Junior +50%, Medium +100%, Senior +200%, all 1hr; Battlefield Super +300% for 5 min); general equipment (rings/bracers, solo +11-25%, set bonuses +10-30%); Spiritual Beast (Behemoth King etc, up to +26% at max level/quality) and Dragon (Celtic Demon +10-20%, Amaterasu Dragon Blade +15% at Lv40, Nidhogg +20% at Lv40).

title: Maximum March Size (by keep level)
summary: Reference march-size totals for keep levels 26-40, broken down by rally spot, research, VIP, rank, gear, item use.
content: Approximate 'typical' max march size by keep level, assuming a normal (not maxed) progression of Rally Spot level, research, VIP, rank, and monarch gear for that keep level, plus optional March Size Increase item use (0/50/100/200%); NOT a true theoretical maximum since VIP/rank/monarch gear can exceed keep-level norms. Examples (no item used / with 200% item): k26 298,200 / 518,200; k28 492,200 / 782,200; k30 772,400 / 1,172,400; k32 971,400 / 1,471,400; k34 1,313,400 / 1,943,400; k36 1,769,500 / 2,539,500; k38 2,326,550 / 3,246,550; k40 3,427,500 / 4,527,500. At higher keep levels the breakdown increasingly includes General Equipment (Fearless Dragon/Ares Ring, Plantagenet Ring), March Size skillbook Lv4 (+12%), Dragon Celtic Demon (Lv6-10), Rally Spot Duty Officer (Special Officer +25%/+50,000, Toyotomi Hideyoshi +5%), Civilization Treasure Imperial Seal, and Ideal Land ornaments. Full derivation and all lever details are in 'How to increase March Size'.

title: How to increase March Speed
summary: 3 march-speed types (basic, to-monster, to-resource) and the free/paid levers that raise each.
content: 3 march speed types: (A) Basic march speed applies everywhere; (B) March Speed to Monsters (also speeds the return trip, even as rally owner/participant); (C) March Speed to Resource Tile. Items: 'March Speed Increase' +20% for 1hr (1,250 Gems, buyable from Store); paid Adv (+40%)/Super (+40-60%) versions via IAP; single-march speed items (+30-50%, stackable) let you strike distant enemies almost instantly. (A) Basic levers: General Leadership (level/enhance/cultivate/dragon), skillbook Lv1-4 (+10-25%), specific generals (Seleucus I +35%, Huo Qubing -20% march time), Covenant (up to +155% applying to ALL generals if multiple covenant generals are maxed), Research (Adv Compass up to +70%, Raid/Adv Raid up to +100% combined), Monarch Gear Courage Horn (Lv6 +33%), Monarch Talent Lv2 pick, Spiritual Beast Thunder Scorpion (up to +30%), 4-beast March Speed Seals (up to +120% combined at Lv20), Dragon (Fasolt +18.8% at Lv40 for 8hrs, Fafnir up to +40% at Lv20 for that general only), Rank (Knight +5% up to Regent +70%), VIP13-25 (+10% to +50%), Art Hall Discobolus (+10% to +60%), Alliance Science donation buff, Victory Column Lv3-5 (requires Keep 35; -10/-20/-30% march time), Coronation Fountain Zeus's Might (up to ~+13%), Champion Josephine/Nefertiti intimacy (up to +60% each, +120% both maxed). (B) March Speed to Monsters levers (separate, larger buffs): skillbook Lv1-4 (+15-45%), Research Tracing/Master Tracker (up to +150% combined), Monarch Gear Justice Horn (Lv6 +66%), 7-beast Seals (up to +296% combined at Lv20), VIP7-25 (+30% to +150%), Art Hall Discobolus (+15% to +120%), Civilization Treasure Tutankhamun's Dagger (Lv1 +10% up to Lv15 +350%), paid subscriptions (Alliance Monthly Card +40%/$19.99/30d, Super Value Privilege Card +15%/$49.99/365d), event-only Blessing of Flame/Pan/Wind (+50%/$4.99/1day), Coronation Fountain Poseidon's Command (up to +30%). (C) To Resource Tile: Research Gathering March Speed (up to +10%) and Adv Gathering March Speed.

title: How to increase the General's Power (and the Danger)
summary: 5 levers that raise a general's power score, plus the key warning that power != actual strength.
content: General power is driven by: (1) Stats (leadership/attack/defense/politics) via leveling up, enhance (star level), Cultivate (up to ~+500 total; gold covers up to ~+300, gems needed beyond that; roughly +1 net stat per 10 Cultivate attempts on average, so ~3,000 Cultivates needed to reach +300), and assigning a dragon (+~6 to +100 depending on dragon level); (2) Specialty, unlocked at general level 25, with 5 quality tiers (Green->Blue->Purple->Orange->Gold) that each add more power; (3) Star Level: below star 5 power rises via stat gains; from star 6+ each red star itself adds a large power jump (e.g. Red Star 1 ~150,000 power, Red Star 2 ~180,000, Red Star 3 ~200,000) independent of stats; (4) Generals' Equipment: pricier gear + star-upgrading (up to 5 stars, 3-10x power) + refining adds the most power (first refine ~1.5x; top-tier Achaemenidae gear can reach +100% buff total per piece via refine); (5) Spiritual Beast and Dragon assignment also raise a general's power (dragon refining adds +60-100% depending on dragon type; power shown on the dragon's own screen does not reflect the boost given to the general). THE DANGER: power is only a rough guide, NOT equal to real strength; skillbook buffs, research buffs, and monarch gear buffs do NOT count toward the power number at all, so a general fully buffed via research/skillbooks can be far stronger than their power score suggests, and conversely a general decked in expensive but wrong-type (unrefined) equipment can have high power yet be weaker than a cheaply-equipped general with correctly-refined, type-matched gear.

title: How to increase the Max Number of Generals you can hold
summary: General storage cap rises with Keep level and with VIP level (using VIP time); limits have been raised repeatedly.
content: The maximum number of generals you can hold (shown atop the Generals screen) is increased by two free levers: (1) Keep level — cap rises as you upgrade the Keep, e.g. Keep 28 -> 50 generals, Keep 29 -> 52, Keep 33 -> 67, Keep 39 -> 104; (2) VIP level plus an active VIP time item — each VIP level adds generals to the cap (VIP1 +0, VIP2 +1, VIP3 +2, ... up to VIP23 +70, VIP24 +75, VIP25 +81); if VIP time lapses, the effective cap drops and you may show as over-capacity (e.g. '75/67') without losing any generals — they remain, you just can't add more until you free slots or VIP time is reactivated. These caps have been raised multiple times via game updates (June 2021, November 2021, April 2024), each time retroactively increasing the limit for existing players.

title: How to get more March Slot
summary: March slots start at 1, max out at 6: +4 from free Academy research, +1 from VIP5+ with VIP time active.
content: March slots (simultaneous marches) start at 1 and cap at 6 total. Free path via Academy research (+4 max, all one-time, no ongoing cost once researched): Military > Formation (requires Academy Lv6) +1; Military > Adv Dispatch (requires Academy Lv13) +1; Military > Super Adv Dispatch (requires Academy Lv25) +1; Military Advance > Legion Expansion (requires Academy Lv35) +1. VIP path: reaching VIP level 5+ AND spending/activating a VIP time item grants +1 march slot while VIP time is active; this bonus does not increase further at any higher VIP level (even VIP20+), it is capped at a flat +1. So the practical maximum is 1 (base) + 4 (research) + 1 (VIP) = 6 march slots.

title: VIP Guide
summary: Overview: VIP level unlocks benefits, earned via VIP Points from in-game activity; benefits require VIP Time to be active.
content: Every player has a VIP Level that rises by earning VIP Points through various in-game activities (see 'How to increase VIP Level & earn VIP Points'). Higher VIP Level unlocks a wide variety of benefits/perks (detailed per-level in 'List of VIP Buffs & Benefits by VIP Level'). Critically, VIP benefits are NOT automatically passive: to actually receive the VIP buffs at your level, your 'VIP Time' must be enabled/active (consumed via VIP time items — see 'How to get VIP Time'); reaching a VIP level alone does not grant its buffs if VIP Time has expired.

title: List of VIP Buffs & Benefits by VIP Level
summary: Full VIP1-28 buff table: VIP5 march slot, VIP8 construction+, VIP10 research+, VIP17 free builder, VIP14+ monster double-drop; buffs need active VIP Time.
content: Full per-level VIP buff table (VIP1-28); VIP buffs apply only while a VIP Time item is active. Economy-relevant thresholds (cumulative, gem-free once VIP level reached): All Resources Production VIP2 +1% -> VIP10 +10% -> VIP15 +20% -> VIP20 +35% -> VIP28 +70%; Free Construction Speed-Up grant scales VIP1 +1min -> VIP7 +10min -> VIP15 +36min -> VIP20 +1:20 -> VIP28 +4:30 (a free periodic speedup source); Construction Speed VIP8 +10% -> VIP15 +18% -> VIP20 +30% -> VIP28 +54%; Crafting Speed from VIP9 +10%; Research Speed VIP10 +10% -> VIP15 +20% -> VIP20 +30% -> VIP28 +46%; Healing Speed from VIP11 +20%; Training Speed VIP12 +10% -> VIP18 +25% -> VIP20 +35% -> VIP24 +45%; March Speed from VIP13 +10%. FREE BUILDER +1 from VIP17 onward (an extra construction queue as long as VIP17+ is active). March Slot +1 at VIP5; March Size Capacity VIP4 +4,000 -> VIP15 +20,000 -> VIP20 +50,000 -> VIP28 +210,000; March Preset Slots 3/4/5/6/7/8 at VIP5/10/13/15/18/20. General Capacity VIP2 +1 -> VIP10 +17 -> VIP15 +34 -> VIP20 +55 -> VIP28 +104. Free daily levies/taxes scale: Gold Levy Times +5(VIP1)->+140(VIP28), Free Tax Times +3(VIP3)->+13(VIP28). Stamina Recovery VIP6 +25% -> VIP15 +50% -> VIP20 +100%; March Speed to Monsters VIP7 +30% -> VIP20 +100% -> VIP25 +150%; Double Items Drop from Monsters VIP14 +10% -> VIP20 +40% -> VIP25 +50%. Subordinate City capacity +1/+2/+3 at VIP15/18/26; VIP13 unlocks Black Market Repurchase (+5/day rebuy); VIP15+ adds combat Atk/Def/HP buffs; VIP27/28 Rally Capacity +6%/+12%. VIP Points required are cumulative and steep: VIP10=77,200, VIP15=1,157,500, VIP20=23,148,200, VIP25=77,160,500, VIP28=240,000,000.

title: How to buy packs at least 10% cheaper
summary: Mostly-defunct method: pay via Amazon Coins for up to 10% off; Amazon Coins and the Aptoide workaround are largely discontinued now.
content: NOTE: this discount method is largely no longer available — Amazon Coins have been discontinued and the Aptoide (AppCoins) workaround is basically unusable now (only some old installs still work). When it worked: buying Amazon Coins in bulk gave a discount (e.g., as of 4/2023, 50,000 coins cost $450 instead of $500 = 10% off; smaller amounts gave 3-8% off), then those coins paid for in-game purchases inside the Amazon Appstore version of Evony (not the regular iOS/Android app).
