Setup required installing 'Amazon Appstore' (Android only, or via an Android emulator like BlueStacks on PC/Mac for iOS players), installing Evony from it, and switching to your existing account via a previously-linked Facebook or Google account. Remaining live tip: for players billed in non-USD currency, per-platform (iOS vs Android) pricing still differs and which is cheaper varies seasonally, so compare both before buying.

title: Paid packages: the best value for which ones to buy and when?
summary: For <$100/month spenders: buy one $4.99 Event Package/day, prioritize Limited Time Promotion + weekend bonus tiers.
content: For budgets under ~$100/month, best value rule of thumb: buy only when a wanted bonus appears in 'Limited Time Promotion'; buy only 'Event Package' or 'Super Value Activity Package' (skip Membership/Bounty Cave, poor value); buy only during the 3-day weekend (extra Weekend Purchase Package bonus); cap spend at one $4.99 pack/day. That's roughly $20/month buying weekly, ~$60/month buying 3x/week. Limited Time Promotion: 4 package types (Speed Up, Resources, Materials & Skills, Research) rotate hourly, in 3 purchase tiers (Tier1 ~1,000 gems/$5, Tier2 ~24,000 gems/$100, Tier3 ~100,000 gems/$400), max 1 of each tier/day; 'Speed Up' is most useful until mid-game, 'Materials and Skills' has the best rate of return (best late-game), 'Resources' is NOT recommended (poor redemption rate). Event Package ($4.99 base: 26,000 Gems, 3M Gold, 23.5M Resources x4 types, ~4 days speedups, resurrection/research/enhancement items) escalates through 5 grades same-day ($4.99->$9.99->$19.99->$49.99->$99.99, each requires buying the prior grade first that day; max 1 of grades 1-4/day, up to 12 of grade 5/day); over time these packages help stockpile Source of Life, Runestone, and Research Stone. Super Value Activity Package (unlocked at 120 daily activity score, 1/day) gives ~15 days speedups, 26,000 Gems, 2.5M Gold, 7.5M Resources x4, but no Source of Life/Runestone — use it when you need speedups urgently. Weekend Purchase Package: bonus tier based on total gems bought that weekend (500 gems->Level1 up to 70,000 gems->Level10); one $4.99 pack reaches Level 2.

title: TIPS for Intentionally Creating Wounded Troops (make as many as you need)
summary: Monster battles cap wounds at 10% per fight; attack with 10x the wounded troops you want and lose on purpose to hit event goals exactly.
content: Core mechanic: in Evony monster/boss battles, a single battle will never wound more than 10% of the troops sent (verified). Exploit: send 10x the number of troops you want wounded and deliberately lose — e.g., to get exactly 200 wounded troops, attack a monster with 2,000 troops and lose on purpose. Use non-ground troop types for a reliable 10% cap (ground-only troops reportedly can wound at less than 10%). If the Monarch Talent 'Mortality' (Lv6) is selected, the wound cap drops to 5%, so you then need 20x the troops instead of 10x. Primary use case: events that reward you for healing a set number of troops — e.g. trade-goods events (Eagle of Freedom, Strong Drink, Shining Treasure, Silk Road, Fragrance, Tea Culture) where healing 30,000 troops yields 300 exchange items, and 200 of those exchange for 500 medals — plus the daily 'heal troops' activity score, and pre-staging wounded troops before events like the Monarch Competition 'Power Increase' stage.

title: What is "Adv Dispatch" ?
summary: Adv Dispatch is an Academy research item (Military category) that grants +1 march slot per level and gates the Keep 14 upgrade.
content: 'Adv Dispatch' is a Military-category research item at the Academy, NOT an auto-gather/auto-dispatch feature — per this source, leveling it up grants +1 march slot per level. It gates progress: you cannot upgrade the Keep to level 14 until 'Adv Dispatch Level 1' is researched (shown in red on the Keep upgrade screen if missing). Requirements to start researching Adv Dispatch: Academy Level 13, 900,000 Gold, and these prerequisite research levels already completed: Conscription Lv2, Swordplay Lv2, Infantry Armor Lv2, Feathered Arrow Lv2, Archer Armor Lv2, Formation Lv1, Horseback Riding Lv3, Cavalry Armor Lv3, Battering Ram Lv3, Siege Machine Armor Lv3, Tracing Lv3, Ground/Mounted/Ranged/Siege Troop HP Lv4 each, and Coordination Lv4. (Note: total march slots — including Adv Dispatch's contribution — are also capped/interact with VIP level; see 'How to get more March Slot' for the full +4 research / +1 VIP breakdown.)

title: Auction House – Product lists, Winning bid prices, etc
summary: Event-only Auction House (in the Market) sells items for GEMS in Mysterious/Rare/Common tiers; only worth it for heavy payers; lost bids refund gems (manually claim).
content: The event-only Auction House (in the Market) sells items for GEMS across three categories (winning-bid examples are server-specific reference only). Mysterious Goods (all servers compete, player-set bids): civilization scroll fragment x30 sets 4.2M-20M gems; Saint Michael Castle 20.4-22M; Famous City Key (Germany) 35-45M. Rare Goods (few servers compete, no buyout): Epic Historic General (Premium) x1 ~150-200k (lowest 53,000); 5K VIP Points x5 lowest 50,000; Senior March Size Increase x1 lowest 55,000; Dragon Scroll Chest x2 lowest 60,000; Achaemenidae Scroll Chest x2 lowest 60,000; Soul Crystal x30 lowest 52,000; 3-Day Speed Up x20 lowest 53,000. Common Goods (auto bid, Buy It Now buyout): 5M resource box x4 = 9,000/16,000; 8h Speed Up x30 = 11,000/51,000; 7-Day Speed Up x3 = 14,600/66,000; 100 Stamina x10 = 16,500/60,000; 1M Gold x8 = 14,600/64,000. Mechanics: Rare/Mysterious let you set bids; Common auto-priced with buyout. A bid in the last 5 minutes resets the deadline to 5 minutes. Gems paid at bid; refunded if you lose but must be manually claimed. For a mostly-F2P bot, the Auction is generally not worth gem spend except opportunistic Common buyouts; note the manual refund-claim step so gems are not lost.

title: Evony: The King's Return GUIDE WIKI
summary: Roster of Evony's recurring limited-time events (category index page).
content: Category listing of recurring Evony events (19 posts), no single timing table given here (see each event's own page for cadence): Bacchus Tavern; Gladiator's Trial quiz; Revelation of Horus; Triumphal Celebration; Hecate's Moon; Battlefield/Clash of Civilizations (CoC); Clash of Civilizations Assault (Shadow of Dawn); Lucky Composing; Treasure Hunt (Pyramid, ~10 days); Revelation of Maya; Alliance Competition (~10 days); Crazy Eggs; Dwarf's Lucky Apple; Arctic Barbarians Invasion; Cleopatra's Treasure; Consuming Return; World Boss; Dawn of Civilization/Monarch Sciences (~10 days, ~2x/month); King's Party/Royal Party (~10 days, ~2x/month); Kill Event (KE). Most limited-time events run about 10 days and several recur roughly twice a month; a bot should track each event's own start/end rather than assume a fixed shared schedule.

title: King’s Party / Royal Party
summary: SPEND/PAY event: buying Basic Gems fills a cake for rewards including Civilization/Duty-Officer scrolls not in normal packs; not F2P-viable -- a whale spend-timing decision, not action-scored.
content: A SPEND/PAY event (~twice a month, ~10 days). Buying gems adds experience to a cake; cake level-ups give rewards. Only BASIC (directly purchased) Gems count -- bonus gems inside packages do NOT (a $4.99 pack counts only its 1,000 basic gems). Cumulative Basic Gems per cake level: Lv2 5,000 (~$20), Lv3 25,000 (~$100), Lv4 50,000 (~$200), Lv5 87,500 (~$350), Lv6 137,500, Lv7 200,000, Lv8 275,000, Lv9 375,000, Lv10 500,000 (~$2,000). Unique for offering things not in normal event packages: Civilization Equipment Scrolls (Lv4 ~$200 and Lv10), Duty Officer Equipment Scroll (Lv8 ~$1,100), plus a buffed decoration and Epic Generals at Lv10. Mechanically like Ekaterina's Garden but far more expensive. Buying 200,000 Basic Gems unlocks the ROYAL Party (next tier, gems carry over): Royal Lv2 238,000 up to Lv7 1,250,000 (~$5,000). NOT F2P-viable -- purely a whale/coiner spend track; the practical target for most is the Lv4 civilization scroll. For a bot: this is a spend-timing event, not an action-scoring event -- no gathering/training/building earns cake experience, only real-money gem purchases. A mostly-F2P bot should ignore it.

title: Treasure Hunt Event (Pyramid)
summary: ~10-day gem-farming event: send a weak march (no general needed) to dive Lv3-5 pyramids for random score; free and low-keep-friendly, up to 189,000 gems at 500M score. Advance the relic-exploration research first.
content: A ~10-day gem-farming event. Pyramids spawn on the world map; you send a march to explore (dive) one, and the score = the items/resources brought back (random). FREE and low-keep-friendly -- no combat; F2P players routinely exceed 2,000M score. Hitting 500M personal score gives 189,000 Gems total across nine chests, plus Senior RSS Chests, healing speedups, Refining Stones, Soul Crystals; top-10 monarch ranking adds up to 300,000 gems. Scoring/high-score tips: (1) Advance Academy research Mysterious Relic Exploration + Adv Mysterious Relic Exploration (Alliance tree) -- higher buff = more brought back per dive (Lv1-5 base has no effect). (2) Dive only Lv3+ pyramids, ideally Lv5; limit 20 dives/day, 20 stamina each, ~15-25 min each. Score ranges: Lv3 400K-5.2M, Lv4 2.4M-29.2M, Lv5 7.4M-88.1M (heavily random). Lv3 x20/day can just reach 500M; Lv5 x20 can yield 180M+/day. (3) Dive ALONE (multiple divers split fewer items = lower score). (4) Send minimal weak troops and NO general -- ~100,000 t1 carrying >=1.3M load is enough; troop level/count/general do NOT change score or time. Find pyramids fast on the PC/Mac client. For a bot, this is a pure gather-style loop (dispatch weak marches to pyramids) that yields the largest single free gem haul; pre-research the relic-exploration nodes.

title: Wisdom Dome
summary: Newer buff building (Keep 28) that unlocks Goddess Attendant troop-type books by level and gives a cumulative all-boost percentage.
content: The Wisdom Dome is a buff building centered on Goddess Attendant merged books: reaching certain levels lets you equip merged books and activate their buffs. Book-unlock levels: Lv2 Charge (Mounted book); Lv5 Precision (Ranged book); Lv9 Formation (Ground book); Lv12 Siegecraft (Siege book); Lv15 Missile (Ranged+Siege book). Separately, upgrading the building grants a cumulative All Goddess Attendant Boosts total scaling with level: +0.5% at Lv1, +1.0% at Lv2, up to +10.0% at Lv15 and +20.0% at Lv25. First level unlocks at Keep 28, Keep-gated upward (Lv4 Keep 29, Lv15 Keep 40, Lv25 Keep 50). Cost F/L/S/O, high speed bonuses (~+170% early, ~+600% late), Power 158,000 (Lv1) to ~55,182,000 (Lv25). Posted 11/2025 (newer building). For a buff-focused bot, Wisdom Dome level both unlocks each troop-type book slot and provides an overall percentage boost to all Goddess Attendant effects. No function is stated as disabled during upgrade.

title: Server Gifts on Wall – Item List
summary: The city walls passively yield a fixed hourly free item (speedups, VIP minutes/points) plus random items when any server player buys a package -- a clean daily-collect target.
content: Free items collected from the city walls -- a daily-collect loop should grab these. Two types: (1) a fixed hourly item; (2) random items triggered when any server player buys a package. Hourly schedule (server time, as of 6/19/2024): 0:00 3h Construction; 1:00 3h Research; 2:00 3h Speed Up; 3:00 8h Construction; 4:00 3h Construction; 5:00 3h Research; 6:00 3h Speed Up; 7:00 8h Speed Up; 8:00 3h Construction; 9:00 12h Healing; 10:00 3h Speed Up; 11:00 8h Research; 12:00 3h Construction; 13:00 12h Speed Up; 14:00 3h Speed Up; 15:00 8h Speed Up; 16:00 3h Construction; 17:00 300 VIP Points; 18:00 3h Speed Up; 19:00 VIP 60 Minutes; 20:00 8h Construction; 21:00 3h Research; 22:00 3h Speed Up; 23:00 8h Construction. So the wall passively yields daily speedups plus a daily VIP-time item (19:00) and VIP points (17:00). Untimed random items (level 5 example): Free Tax x10, 30-min Speed Up x5, 10K resource boxes, VIP 60 minutes x5, etc. Higher wall levels give more/different items. For a bot, a wall-collect action a few times per day harvests free construction/research/general speedups and VIP time -- directly funding base_dev and daily loops.

title: Dawn of Civilization / Monarch Sciences – How to complete all quests
summary: 10-day quest event (~twice/month) -- the cheapest route to the PvP 'march effect' and a golden historic general; 40 quests are largely normal bot actions (gathering, training, healing, reviving, shrine offers).
content: A 10-day event (~twice a month), the cheapest way to get the march effect and a golden historic general. Earn points via quests; rewards every 100 points across 50 levels. All 50 levels need paying a fee AND completing all 40 quests; the $4.99 and $19.99 yellow plans give the same rewards but $19.99 grants 1.5x points. This guide shows completing all 40 quests WITHOUT the $19.99 charge (largely F2P). You can alternatively buy levels with gems (10,400/level; 520,000 for all 50). Rewards feature a march effect (PvP-valuable -- hides general icon when marching), avatar frames, and a golden historic general (Aethelflaed, Pyrrhus, Merlin, Marcus Agrippa, Maurice de Saxe, Raimondo, Narses, Mark Antony). 40 quests in 3 categories: A) Gathering (1,620 pts) -- Resource Gathering, General Cultivate, Equipment Refine, Resource Production (each up to 230), Offer in Shrine 100x = 140; B) Alliance -- Rally Participation, Donate, Help speedups; C) Ambition -- Troop Training (make many T1), Troop Healing (heal 80,000; lose with T1 on purpose), Revive Troop soul in Holy Palace (80,000; needs Holy Palace Lv25+), Recall deserters (100,000), Increase Power (70M), Resource Plundering (18M). Stamina quest: consume 2,400 over 10 days (240/day). Top-100 rankers also get Senior March Size Increase. KEY for a bot: most of these are normal base_dev/daily_collect/gather actions, so time gathering, training, refining, healing, reviving and shrine offers to this event to complete quests nearly free.

title: Art Hall: Exhibition Hall Reward List
summary: The ten Art Hall Exhibition Halls each hold 4 artworks and pay three reward tiers (20/60/200 items) of gems, stamina, speedups, research stones and teleporters once collected.
content: Ten Exhibition Halls in the Art Hall, each filled with 4 artworks (from Artwork Fragment Chests), give 3 progress tiers requiring 20/60/200 items (280 total). Tier-3 rewards (all free once artworks collected): 1. Knight Equipment Hall (Art Hall Lv11+) -> 1K Gems x5, 500K Food x15, 24h Speed Up x15. 2. Oil Painting Hall (Lv13+) -> Research Stone x100, 100K Food x15, 12h Research x15. 3. Stringed Instrument Hall (Lv15+) -> 100 Stamina x5, 100K Lumber x20, 12h Research x20. 4. Jewelry Hall (Lv16+) -> Arrest Warrant x3, 100K Stone x15, 24h Speed Up x15. 5. Noble Crown Hall (Lv17+) -> Research Stone x60. 6. Ancient Coin Hall (Lv19+) -> 200 Gems x5. 7. Body Sculpture Hall (Lv21+) -> 500 Chips x5, 100K Gold x12, 12h Crafting x12. 8. Pot Hall (Lv23+) -> Research Stone x60, 12h Construction x10. 9. Cup Hall (Lv24+) -> 1K Gems x4, 200K Gold x20, 12h Speed Up x15. 10. Mask Hall (Lv25-30) -> Adv City Teleporter x5, 500K Gold x15, 500K Food x15. Activating Mona Lisa (Oil Painting + Jewelry + Ancient Coin halls) grants resource-gathering buffs. For a bot, filling these halls (via Artwork Fragment Chests from Hydra/Crazy Eggs) yields a large one-time payout of gems, stamina, speedups and gathering buffs.

title: Limited Time Promotion – All Package Contents
summary: Paid progressive package (three levels, bought with gems 1,000/24,000/100,000) delivering ~594 days of speedups, resources, materials and EXP; a spend-value pack, not action-scored.
content: A PAID progressive package appearing at higher Keep levels, three levels costing Basic Gems: Lv1 1,000 gems, Lv2 24,000, Lv3 100,000. Aggregate contents across Lv1-3: ~594 days Speed Up; Resources ~167M Food + 165M each Lumber/Stone/Ore; 165 Free Tax; ~1.22M Monarch + 1.22M General EXP (Resources section) plus more in Materials; Materials & Skill: 95 Material Bag (Silk Road), 22 Lv4 Skill Book Chest, 110 Lv4 Dragon Scale, 2,250 Refining Stone, 61 Persia King's Treasure, 61 Pharaoh's Treasure; Research: 135M Gold, 600 Research Stone, ~137 days Research Speedup. Completing all three grants a bonus (60 Material Bag, 30 Lv4 Skill Book Chest, 100 Lv4 Dragon Scale, 1,000 Refining Stone, 60M Gold, ~169 days Speed Up). Lv4 Skill Books cannot be bought in-game with gems. For a bot's spend logic, this is a paid value pack (buying requires spending gems), best treated per the linked paid-package value guide; not an action-scoring event -- no gathering/training/building earns it. A mostly-F2P bot should not prioritize it.

title: Lucky Composing Event
summary: Fully F2P card event: collect 5 colors of cards free from tiles and monsters, combine 3 into Lucky Boxes (10 tiers); use the surplus-minimizing combine algorithm before the event ends.
content: A FREE collect-and-combine (card gacha) event. You obtain composite items (cards) from resource tiles and monsters -- both normal bot actions, so time gathering and monster-hunting to the window. There are 5 card colors (Green, Blue, Purple, Orange, Red). Combining 3 cards makes a Lucky Box; there are 10 Lucky Box tiers, higher = more luxurious (contents vary per event, shown only in screenshots). F2P-viable -- cards come purely from gathering/hunting; reward quality depends on which box tier you compose. Because leftover cards are wasted, the page gives an algorithm: (1) select the three most-numerous colors, (2) composite about half the maximum possible, (3) repeat -- remainders occur if the total is not a multiple of 3. A simulation tool computes the combinations consuming the most cards. Commenters note occasional glitches (only one box of each number, or slider resets) fixed by rebooting. No scoring/ranking mechanic -- value is simply the boxes assembled from freely gathered cards. For a bot: the loop is gather + hunt during the event -> accumulate cards -> combine into the highest boxes possible before the event ends; gathering speedups are also a listed reward source elsewhere.

title: Crazy Eggs
summary: Fully F2P hammer event: spend hammers to smash/activate four eggs (min ~58, usually ~70 for all four) for gems, stamina, teleporters, speedups, materials and artwork chests; time hammer-earning to it.
content: A FREE hammer-driven reward event. You spend Hammers to smash four numbered eggs; smashing enough activates an egg for a big bonus. Hammer costs to activate: Princess Lucy 3, James Bowie 6, Isabella I 8, Minamoto no Yoshitsune 12. Activating all four auto-activates a fifth Caesar Egg. Each egg must be smashed at least twice, so all four needs a minimum (3+6+8+12)x2 = 58 hammers; ~70 usually suffices (luck-dependent). Hammers come from the normal hammer methods, so time hammer-earning to the event. Each smash randomly gives 4 of 5 items; activation + Caesar's egg fill the rest. Priority if short on hammers: Gems smash Minamoto (12), Stamina smash James Bowie (6), Arrest Warrant smash Isabella (8), Refining Stones smash James Bowie (6). F2P-viable. Total value when all eggs activated (minimum): ~106h general speedups plus training 196h/healing 84h/construction, research, crafting, trap 36h each; Food/Lumber 9.5M, Stone/Ore 6.5M boxes; Gem 10,800, Gold 5M, Chip 3,000; 100 Stamina x4, Adv City Teleporter x6, Arrest Warrant x6, Adv Troop March Speedup x4; Material Lv5 x38, Refining Stone x540, Runestone x15, Research Stone x32, Dragon Crystal x33, Magic Artwork Chest x135. Tip: break eggs early -- regen/4-hour timers can make you miss the window. For a bot, this is a major free source of gems, stamina, teleporters and artwork chests -- stockpile hammers and dump them here.

title: Drop Rate of Historic General at Tavern (In-game displayed values & Test results)
summary: Tavern level directly sets historic-general odds; free daily tavern check is worth doing, Lv24+ best for gem-refresh.
content: Tavern level 10 or below never yields historic generals (only Common/Uncommon). Historic-general (Legendary/Epic) odds rise with tavern level; per-6-slot-refresh chance (official in-game values, confirmed close to real test data): at Lv30, ~7.32% for a Legendary historic and ~2.51% for an Epic historic; at Lv37 (max tested), ~9.76%/3.38%. Real mass-refresh test at Lv30 (3,400 refreshes/170,000 gems) measured ~2.2-2.5% gold-historic rate per refresh, matching in-game figures within margin of error, though probability appeared to dip slightly the more refreshes were done in one sitting and certain generals clustered in bursts. Guidance: raise Tavern to at least Lv24+ before spending gems seriously chasing historic generals; even free/no-cost natural refreshes (checking tavern periodically) can yield a historic general (several users report free pulls at Lv10-20); cap serious gem-refresh sessions at ~100/day and try a different day if unlucky rather than over-spending in one sitting.

title: Alliance Competition Event
summary: ~10-day quest event where many NORMAL bot actions (building, research, gathering, boss kills, cultivating, shrine offers) score points; delete low-value quests and time your actions to it. Free to participate.
content: A ~10-day event where you complete quests for a personal monarch score; the sum of members' scores drives alliance-score/ranking rewards, and maxing your score grants a generals redemption item. Score to MAX by alliance rank: Novice 2,600 / Junior 3,000 / Elite 3,500 / Legendary 4,300 / Epic 5,200. Challenges (quest starts) are capped and grow with alliance rank (Elite = 17 free); up to 5 more can be bought with Gems. Cancelling a started quest does NOT refund a challenge. Quests are Personal or Alliance (shared, first-come); R4+ can delete unwanted alliance quests. Each quest gives 50-240 points by difficulty. KEY: many normal bot actions score -- Increase power (up to 25M=+240), Increase building power (City Development, 300K=+240), Increase research power (450K=+240), Cultivate Generals (500x=+240), Consume Runestones (1.5K=+240), Refine equipment, Gather RSS outside city (54M one-type=+240), Produce/claim resources in city, Kill Boss Monsters (80x=+240), Kill Lv14+ bosses, Consume Stamina (2K=+170), Offer at Shrine (80x=+240), Spin Wheel of Fortune (360=+170), Levy Gold, Donate, Consume Alliance Points, Consume Gems (500K=+240). Tips: since challenges are capped, R4/R5 delete low quests so only 140+ (170+ for Elite+) remain; aim for 200/240 quests. Easy wins: dump saved runestones, offer stacked tributes at once, join boss rallies, spend surplus Wheel chips. FREE-to-participate. For a bot, this is the flagship event to synchronize base_dev (building/research power), gather, training and daily-collect actions to.

title: When is Server Merges ? & What happens?
summary: Irregular (~1-2x/year) merges, 10-day advance notice; personal assets kept but rankings/relics/bookmarks reset.
content: Server merges happen irregularly at Evony's discretion (roughly once or twice a year historically), based on server environment, active player count, and availability of a matching server; notification email goes out ~10 days before merge, maintenance details the day before, merge completes within a few hours of maintenance. Effects for bot planning: personal data is preserved (building levels, VIP level, gems, gold, resources, sub cities, generals, troops, items); alliance remains but alliance buildings are removed (rebuild is free if already built pre-merge); players are randomly teleported onto the new shared map; all resource tiles and monsters reset and relics are removed; field troops auto-return to city; SvS/Battlefield rankings, Kings/Titles, and Monarch Competition all reset (last-stage rewards still sent; Throne War restarts after 24h); map bookmarks are deleted; duplicate names get numbered. A bot should re-scout resource tiles/relics and re-bookmark targets right after any merge.

title: Server Time vs Country Time Table (US, UK, RU, JP)
summary: Server time runs on UTC; daily reset is at server 0:00, shifting by 1 hour locally under non-DST winter time.
content: Evony server time is UTC-based (displayed at the top of the options screen). Conversion table given for USA Los Angeles (PDT UTC-7 / PST UTC-8), USA New York (EDT UTC-4 / EST UTC-5), UK (BST UTC+1 summer / GMT UTC+0 winter), Russia Moscow (MSK UTC+3 year-round), and Japan (JST UTC+9 year-round, no DST). During DST changeover (2nd Sunday March - 1st Sunday November per US convention), the daily in-game reset (tied to server midnight) effectively shifts by 1 hour in local clock time for countries/regions that do not observe daylight saving. SvS (Server vs Server) event timing does NOT change with DST; Kill Event (KE) timing DOES change with DST. Useful for a bot scheduling gathering/build/kill actions around fixed server-time daily resets and event windows regardless of the operator's local timezone.

title: Rally Spot
summary: Controls your own march/rally size (troops per march); Keep-gated and Army-Camp-gated from Lv26; Duty Officer slot at Lv35 increases March Size Capacity.
content: The Rally Spot governs your own march size / rally capacity (troops per march). Builds Lv1-50, each level requiring the matching Keep level; from Lv26 additionally the matching Army Camp level (Army Camp 26 at Lv26 ... 50 at Lv50), so Army Camp gates Rally Spot from Lv26 up. No troop-tier Unlock column. Key milestone: Duty Officer at Lv35 (increases March Size Capacity; ideal Toyotomi Hideyoshi, Niwa Nagahide, Zhang Liang). March size is raised through multiple systems beyond this building (12 ways to increase March Size, max march size by Keep level, and March Size Increase items). Costs: Lv1 780 food/780 lumber; Ore at Lv12; Lv25 ~8.5M/11M/11M/11M; Lv50 ~36.6B/45.7B/45.6B/45.7B. Power 8 to 35,000,000. Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610%. For a bot, Rally Spot level is a primary lever for solo march/gather size (bigger marches = more resources per gather haul); prioritize Lv35 for the March-Size duty officer, and keep Army Camp leveled to unblock Rally Spot Lv26+.

title: Holy Palace
summary: Recalls Deserters and revives dead Souls (separate from the Hospital); soul revival consumes Soul Crystals; gates Keep 38 and the Victory Column.
content: At the Holy Palace you recall Deserters and revive dead soldiers that became Souls (distinct from the Hospital's wounded/SvS dead). Higher level increases deserter/soul capacity, horn production speed, and the dead-to-soul conversion rate. Buildable at Keep 11. The Holy Palace must be raised first to raise the Keep to 38 or to build/raise the Victory Column. All menus stay usable during upgrade (soul revival and deserter recall keep working). Milestones: Lv25 unlocks Troop Soul Revival (ability to revive souls); Lv35 Duty Officer (increases max deserter capacity and war-dead-to-soul conversion rate, and decreases resources needed to revive souls; ideal Helena, then Trivia Hecate). Soul revival consumes Soul Crystals (separate guide). Keep-gated 1:1, cost F/L/S/O, speed to +610% by Lv50, Power ~107M at Lv50. For a bot, the two automatable actions are recalling deserters and reviving souls, both gated by capacity that scales with level plus duty officer; both count toward some events (Dawn of Civilization, Alliance Competition).

title: Forge
summary: Equipment-crafting building (Keep 7); higher level and Watchtower level unlock higher equipment tiers; Craft is blocked while it upgrades; Champion's/King's sets give gathering and double-drop bonuses.
content: The Forge crafts generals' equipment; stronger gear needs higher Forge level plus Materials and Scrolls. Buildable at Keep 7; cannot reach Lv26+ unless the Watchtower is also leveled. Civilization Equipment is crafted at the Wonder (needs Keep 33), not the Forge. For automation: Craft CANNOT be selected during a Forge upgrade; Equipment Upgrade unlocks at Lv30; Duty Officer at Lv28 (increases Craft speed; ideal Hugh O'Donnell, then Ono no Imoko). Equipment-tier milestones: Lv5 Purple; Lv9 Orange; Lv13 Gold Champion's (4-piece set adds a gathering bonus -- valuable for gatherers); Lv17 Gold General's; Lv21 Gold King's (King's Ring gives cost-effective double-item drop -- key for hammer/gold/medal farming); Lv27 Red Dragon + duty-officer equipment; Lv30 Red Ares/Imperial; Lv33 Red Achaemenidae/Parthian (Achaemenidae key for subordinate-city-mayor debuffs at k33+). Crafting speed is raised via Academy Blacksmith research, Agile Crown monarch gear and a duty officer. For a bot's economy, prioritize the Champion's (gathering) and King's (double-drop) gold sets which directly boost resource, hammer, gold and medal yields.

title: Council of State
summary: PvP buff building (from Keep 1, but gated by Pasture/Victory Column) where appointing star-6+ golden generals to positions grants combat buffs; unlocks more positions by level.
content: The Council of State grants new buffs by appointing generals to positions; higher level unlocks more positions. Buildable at Keep 1 but requires the Pasture and Victory Column raised first (rows gated by Pasture 27-35 and Victory Column 1-10). Menus (Senate, Soul Binding) available from start; Soul Binding links two generals to inherit specialty/general level. Each level adds to total Generals Capacity. Position-unlock milestones: Lv1 Governor>Ground; Lv4 Governor>Mounted; Lv7 Governor>Ranged; Lv10 Governor>Siege; Lv13 Commander>Ground ... Lv30 Marshal>Siege. Six Military Titles x four troop-type positions = 24 positions; Senator-tier positions unlock via Server War/Gaugamela/Constantinople achievements at Council Lv8. Buffs come from three levers: promoting Military Title grade, leveling a Position (Merit Jade + Glory Golden Twig), and appointing star-6+ generals. Appointment needs a star-5+ general (golden historic only -- purple cannot be appointed); appointed generals are locked from all other uses. For a buff-automation bot this is a major PvP combat-buff source gated behind Pasture/Victory Column progression, low priority for pure economy.

title: Prison
summary: Converts PvP captives into resources (Labor) or Prestige (Release); level raises Prison capacity and capture rate; imprison generals unlocked at Keep 15.
content: The Prison holds captives (soldiers and generals) taken in PvP. Captured SOLDIERS -> Labor (yields resources equal to 4 hours of your city's BASE production only, excluding research/green bonuses; consumes 1,000 captives, 6h cooldown endable with Gems) or Release (yields 50 Prestige fixed regardless of Prison level; consumes 1,000 captives, 6h cooldown, not shortenable). Captured GENERALS: leave imprisoned (released for Gold if the enemy pays Gems, or auto-freed after a time limit), Release unconditionally, or Execute (dead general then revived at the Shrine). Higher Prison level increases Prison Capacity and Captive Rate. Buildable at Keep 11 (also needs Quarry; early levels Quarry-gated, raise Quarry first). Imprison menu unlocks at Keep 15, and generals can only be captured if both sides are Keep 15+. All menus usable during upgrade. Captives come only from PvP wins between server players or SvS enemy-server fights (not NPC sub-cities, barbarians or battlefields). If your main city is broken into, all imprisoned generals are released. Keep-gated, Power ~62M at Lv50. For an economy bot, the Prison is a minor PvP-derived resource/prestige source, not a core economy lever.

title: Triumphal Arch
summary: End-game buff building (Keep 38, achievement-gated) giving rally/reinforcement capacity and battlefield combat buffs; gated behind Art Hall and Ideal Land.
content: The Triumphal Arch is a buff building -- raising its level provides rally/reinforcement and battlefield buffs. Buildable at Keep 38, and the Keep cannot reach 39 unless the Arch is built (Lv1). The Art Hall must be raised first; the Arch cannot exceed Lv5 unless Ideal Land is Lv7+ (Lv6 needs Ideal Land 7, Lv7 needs Ideal Land 8). Buffs by level (Lv1-7): Rally Capacity +3%->+21%; Reinforcement Capacity +3%->+21%; Rally Capacity in Battlefield +10%->+70%; Reinforcement Capacity in Battlefield +10%->+60%; Troop Attack/Defense/HP in Battlefield from Lv3 (+10%) to +50% at Lv7. All menus (Glory Achievement, Monarch Battle Logs) usable during upgrade. Upgrade requirements are achievement-gated: Lv1 needs Art Hall 38; higher levels need Gaugamela/Constantinople score thresholds, All-Star placement, consecutive wins, and (Lv6-7) Ideal Land 7/8 plus server-war personal-point milestones. Resource costs are very large. For a buff-automation bot, this is a high-end rally/reinforcement and battlefield-combat buff source gated behind Keep 38, Art Hall and Ideal Land; low priority for economy-first play.

title: Victory Column
summary: Buff building (Keep 35) giving Construction Speed, Research Speed, March-Time Reduction and general-attribute buffs; upgrade requires owning high-power generals rather than mainly resources.
content: The Victory Column is a buff building. Buildable at Keep 35, and the Keep cannot reach 36 unless the Column is built (Lv1). It must be raised to level up the Dragon Cliff and Council of State; raising it requires the Holy Palace first plus owning powerful generals. Buffs by level (Lv1-11): General's basic attributes +1%->+11%; Construction Speed +5%->+80%; Research Speed +5%->+80%; March Time Reduction from Lv3 (+10%) to +42% at Lv11. Important: the General's basic-attribute buffs (Leadership/Attack/Defense/Politics) from the Column COUNT toward duty-officer appointment requirements (unlike dragon-derived buffs). All menus usable during upgrade. Upgrade requirements are unusual -- instead of only resources, each level demands owning several high-power generals and/or generals with maxed specialties (Lv1 needs Holy Palace 35 + two 5.8M-power generals; higher levels up to 18 Epic generals at star 10 and 11M-power generals; Lv11 needs Academy 45). The maxed-specialties requirement can be met with Purple Historic generals (only 3 needed). For an economy/buff bot, the Column's Construction Speed +80%, Research Speed +80% and March-Time Reduction are strong base_dev accelerators once reachable.

title: Archer Tower
summary: Wall-defense building (Keep 6, Walls-gated); Duty Officer slot very early at Lv11; gates high-level Archer Camp upgrades.
content: The Archer Tower is a wall-defense building. Builds Lv1-50, each level requiring Keep 6 plus a matching Walls level for early tiers (Walls 2 at Lv2 through Walls 50 at Lv50); from Lv7 also the matching Keep level. No Unlock column, so no troop-tier/Blazon unlocks. Key milestone: Duty Officer at Lv11 -- the earliest duty-officer slot among these buildings (increases Archer Tower Attack, i.e. wall-defense damage; ideal Ban Chao, Themistocles, Maurice of Nassau). Costs: Lv1 900 food/900 lumber; Ore at Lv12; Lv25 ~9.8M/12M/13M/13M; Lv50 ~35B/44.2B/44.2B/45.7B. Power 15 (Lv1) to 70,000,000 (Lv50). Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610% at Lv50. The Archer Camp requires matching Archer Tower levels for several upgrades (Lv27/30/32/35 and every level 36-50), so Archer Tower level is a prerequisite for high-level Archer Camp progression. For a bot: treat as a wall-defense asset; the cheap Lv11 duty-officer slot is an easy early buff, and keep it leveled to unblock Archer Camp upgrades.

title: War Hall
summary: Governs host-rally capacity (participants you can accept when you host a rally); Keep 9 to start, Embassy-gated; no duty officer.
content: The War Hall increases rally capacity for rallies you host: higher level raises the max participants you can accept when you start (host) your own rally. Buildable at Keep 9. Leveling requires first raising the Embassy -- each level from Lv2 requires the matching Embassy level (Embassy 2 at Lv2 ... 50 at Lv50), and higher levels also the matching Keep level. No Duty Officer section and no troop-tier Unlock column. All menus stay usable during upgrades. It also provides the Alliance War screen (monitor rallies/scouts by alliance members or enemies -- same as Alliance > Alliance War). Costs: Lv1 1,000 food/1,000 lumber; Ore at Lv12; Lv25 ~11M/14M/15M/14M; Lv50 ~52.5B/66.6B/68.6B/66.6B. Power 10 to 72,000,000. Speed +0% to Lv17, +30% at Lv18 to +610%. The Workshop requires matching War Hall levels for Lv36-50 upgrades, so War Hall level is a prerequisite for high-level siege progression. For a bot: the host-rally-capacity building, gated behind Embassy (and Keep 9 to start), distinct from the Rally Spot (your own solo march size).

title: Important Usage of Subordinate City
summary: Subordinate cities (Keep 11) give buffs/debuffs/general EXP by culture, not their own troops; Korea boosts gathering+warehouse, China boosts production+training, America boosts sub-city gold+research; up to 9 held.
content: Subordinate cities become available at Keep 11. Their major benefits are buffs, debuffs and general EXP -- not their own gold/troops. Buffs apply just by owning a sub-city, scale with rarity, and stack across cities of the same culture; occupying sub-cities on the map grants an additional buff per city. Buffs depend on culture. Battle cultures: Japan = main-city attacking-troop attack (marching buff) + Research Factory material speed; Russia = in-city troop attack + trap attack; Arabia = hospital capacity + healing speed. Development cultures: Korea = extra resources from gathering + warehouse capacity; Europe = construction speed + general EXP from monster kills; China = main-city resource production + training speed; America = current sub-city gold production + research speed. Concrete numbers: hold up to 9 sub-cities; 9 Japanese Epics = +90% attack; 9 Korean Epics = +135% resource-gathering (finishing a 7M tile yields 16.45M). The in-city sub-city buff differs from the Culture Feature buff. Change the in-city sub-city culture by changing your main city's culture. Debuffs: sub-city troops are weak -- instead assign a mayor general with debuff skills (attack/HP down), which stack across mayors (some reach -600% with equipment/specialty); Ares/Achaemenidae debuff equipment and the Norway Ridge dragon amplify this. Mayors earn half the main general's EXP. For an economy bot, Korea (gathering+warehouse), China (production+training) and America (sub-city gold+research) are the development-culture picks; combined with Champion's gear and gathering research they multiply the free gathering lever.

title: How to get Hammer efficiently
summary: Hammers (for Crazy Eggs) come free from double-drop boss hunts (~50% drop) and gathering ~Lv6 tiles; aim ~20-30/day; a full four-egg activation needs ~60-70 hammers.
content: Hammers smash Crazy Eggs; activating all four eggs needs ~60-70 hammers (up to ~100 if unlucky); goal ~20-30/day. All methods free/F2P. Two main: (1) Hunt bosses with a Double-Drop general -- drops a hammer ~50% of the time; low-level bosses drop at the same rate, so farm the map edge for fast low-level bosses (survey: 44 hammers from 85 bosses, 51%). Vikings drop worse (~28%). (2) Gather resource tiles ~level 6 -- a hammer is available when a gathering completes; probability roughly proportional to tile level. A ~Lv6 tile finishes in ~1-2 hours with ~70% hammer chance; better to gather several small tiles quickly than a few long high-level ones (high tiles take 5-12h). To raise availability, maximize gather speed: high-Politics general, gathering-speed skills (Lucy), Monarch Gear crystals (attach before reaching tile, removable after arrival), and the all-resources gathering-speed city-buff item. Comments (unverified): Lv7+ tiles reportedly give 100% hammers; some farm map-edge Lv2-3 tiles with 5 march threads. Hammers do not drop from normal (non-boss) monsters. For a bot, a King's-Ring double-drop general on fast low-level boss hunts, plus a gathering fleet, produces steady hammers to feed Crazy Eggs during event windows.

title: How to get Stamina
summary: Stamina refills to 100 at daily reset and recovers faster with VIP/talent; the huge free source is the Consuming Return Event, with Crazy Eggs and Lucky Apples secondary.
content: Stamina refills to 100 at daily server reset; recovery speed increases with VIP level and Monarch Talent Lv18. Best free method: Resource Consuming Return Event (needs ~Keep 25+): per resource type, 125M consumed = 100 Stamina x2, 500M = x4, 1,250M = x8, 2,500M = x12, 5,000M = x20; consuming 2,500M across 4 types = 104 pieces of 100-stamina. Otherwise the only relatively good options are Crazy Eggs and Lucky Apples; the rest are small lucky drops. Crazy Egg: smash 6-hammer (James Bowie) egg -> 100 Stamina x4 if lucky. Dwarf's Lucky Apple Blessing (5 wishing coins) -> 100 Stamina x1 if lucky. Free/event sources: Trade Products Event 100x3; King's Path Day2; Login Rewards Lv3 Witch's Treasure Bag; Garuda Trial (10/30/50 kills -> 10/50/100); Lucky Composing boxes; Tavern (25 Stamina x1, 3x/day at 3:30/9:30/12:30 server time); Wheel of Fortune; Arch of Triumph flower rewards. Paid/gem: Shopping Spree, Store, Black Market (50 = 300 gems), Alliance Shop (discount only), packages. For a bot, stamina gates monster/boss hunting (the main free source of speedups, hammers, medals, materials), so top it up daily via the tavern and time Consuming Return events for bulk stamina.

title: How to get Badge (& Useage)
summary: Badges (Battlefield Shop currency for generals, Wonder, Ares equipment, dragon training) come mainly free from Battle of Constantinople and Battle of Chalons; NOT from Gaugamela.
content: Badges are spent at the Battlefield Shop (Historic General = 2,040), to unlock/upgrade the Wonder (Lv1/2/3 = 1,700/2,800/4,800), upgrade Dragon->Ares equipment (800/piece), and raise a dragon (~50/feeding, 5/refine + 50 refining stones). Ares equipment and dragon training need huge quantities, so shop carefully. Acquisition is mainly FREE via Battle of Constantinople (BoC) -- NOT available at Battle of Gaugamela. Participating gives at least 180 badges, scaling with win/loss and personal+alliance score; DOUBLED during special battlefield periods (every few months). Personal-score badges (11/22/2024): 1800+ = 620 (win)/500 (lose); 1200-1799 = 570/450; 800-1199 = 480/370; 500-799 = 400/280; 250-499 = 310/230; <=249 = 250/180. Alliance-score bonus: 100 = 10, 250 = 26, 500 = 50, 1200 = 90, 2500 = 150. Battle of Chalons: at least 200 badges, by team ranking and personal score. Paid: the 25000%-return 5th-level pack ($99.99) includes 100 badges. Royal Chest (all three Royal Thieves) may give 5-10 badges. Comment (unverified): some high-level bosses can drop badges. For a bot, badges are a battlefield-participation currency -- earn them by joining BoC/Chalons, and spend on the Wonder, key generals and Ares/dragon progression.

title: How to get Scroll
summary: Scrolls craft mid-to-high equipment; General's/King's/Dragon scroll chests drop free from specific bosses/Vikings, but Achaemenidae, Duty-Officer and Civilization scrolls are largely event-only and heavily paid.
content: Scrolls craft mid-to-high equipment in Forge/Wonder; not sold in Store or Alliance Shop. General's Scroll Chest (free): lucky drop from Lv8 Peryton, Lv9 Minotaur, Royal Thief, Junior Cerberus; or Black Market 6,075 gems. King's Scroll Chest: from Lv10 Griffin, Lv11 Ifrit, Junior Cerberus; Black Market 18,230 gems; Battlefield Shop 360 badges / 1,140 crystals. Dragon Scroll Chest: from Lv12 Kamaitachi, Lv13 Fafnir, Lv14 Behemoth, Lv15 Phoenix, Viking Icebreaker, Medium/Senior Cerberus, Excellent Garuda; Event Pack Tier 5 (2 pcs); Black Market 54,000 gems; Battlefield Shop 1,160 badges / 3,420 crystals; Auction (event only). Achaemenidae Scroll Chest: from Fafnir, Behemoth, Phoenix, Viking Berserker/Chief, Medium/Senior Cerberus; Event Pack Tier 5; Auction only. Duty Officer Equipment Scrolls and Civilization Equipment Scrolls are event-only and heavily PAID. Civilization scrolls assemble from 30 fragments via Conquest (low-difficulty) or Supremacy (high) chests; the Wheel of Civilization is technically free but expected ~$300 (low)/$1,600 (high); Noble Coins from monster hunting (90/day cap), gathering (10/day) or packages. Astrolabe: pay-only. King's Party: Lv4 cake = 1 low scroll (~$200), Lv10 = 1 high scroll (~$2,000). For a bot, farm the free General's/King's/Dragon scroll chests from bosses/Vikings; treat Achaemenidae/Civilization scrolls as paid/end-game.

title: How to get Adv Troop March Speedup
summary: Adv Troop March Speedup (PvP must-have) comes free mainly from the Gather Troops event (build Lv6+ traps) and Golden Refining (cultivate generals); Store price 1,000 gems -- avoid.
content: Adv Troop March Speedup is a must-have PvP item (20+ used per BoG/server war); Store price 1,000 gems each -- get it free via events. Best free method: Gather Troops Event Day 4 Defense Force (up to 51 pcs) by building traps Lv6+ (needs Trap Factory Lv26+): 1,000 traps = 1, 2,000 = 3, 3,000 = 5, 4,000 = 7, 5,000 = 9 (25 total), 10,000 = 11 (36 total), 50,000 = 15 (51 total). Golden Refining Event Cultivate Generals (up to 40 pcs): cultivate 10/30/80/150 times -> 1/2/3/4 pcs (10/day over 4 days). Crazy Eggs: smash 12-hammer Minamoto no Yoshitsune egg -> 4+/day if lucky. Less efficient: Trade Products Event -> 2 pcs; Dwarf's Lucky Apple Blessing -> 10 if lucky; Viking Challenge lucky; Wheel of Fortune lucky. Comment: Gather Troops may have been replaced by War Preparation. For a bot, the March Speedup is a PvP consumable -- build traps during Gather Troops and cultivate generals during Golden Refining to stockpile it free.

title: How to get March Size Increase Item
summary: March Size Increase items (Junior/Medium/Senior) come free from Viking chests, Dawn of Civilization and World Boss rankings, and Faberge-Egg tile drops; otherwise expensive gems.
content: March Size Increase items come in Junior/Medium/Senior tiers. Free/event sources: Viking Event Viking War Chest may give any tier (lucky). Dawn of Civilization top ranking: 1st = Senior x10, 2nd = Senior x6, 3rd = Senior x3, 4th-100th = Senior x2. World Boss personal-damage ranking: 1st = Senior x3, 2nd = Senior x2, 3rd-10th = Senior x1, 11th-50th = Medium x1, 51st-100th = Junior x1. Tsarevich Egg (needs Civilization Treasure Faberge Egg Lv2+): from Lv10+ resource tiles, may give one Medium if lucky. Maria Egg (Faberge Egg Lv3+): from Lv15+ tiles, may give one Senior if lucky. Paid: Auction Rare Goods Senior lowest 55,000 gems; Store Senior 100,000 / Medium 25,000 / Junior 8,000 gems; Event Package 4th ($49.99) = Senior x1, 5th ($99.99) = Senior x3; Battlefield Shop Battlefield Super March Size Increase = 25,000 gems. For a bot, march size directly raises troops per gather/attack; earn increases free via rankings (Dawn of Civilization, World Boss) and Faberge-Egg tile drops rather than paying.

title: How to get Artwork Fragment Chest
summary: Artwork Fragment Chests (I-VI) fill the Art Hall Exhibition Halls; best free source is Magic Artwork Chests from Lv1 Junior Hydra (100%) and Crazy Eggs.
content: Artwork Fragment Chests (I-VI) fill the Art Hall Exhibition Halls; Chests V and VI (for Knight Equipment and Mask halls) are hardest. Best free strategy: smash lots of Crazy Eggs and farm Lv1 Junior Hydra to collect Magic Artwork Chests (each gives a random Artwork Fragment Chest I-VI). Free sources per tier: Chest I from Lv4-6 tiles (lucky), normal monsters Lv11-21, Black Market 5/1,000 gems. Chest II from Lv7-10 tiles, monsters Lv19-30, Black Market 5/1,500. Chest III from Lv11-12 tiles, monsters Lv31-35, normal bosses Lv1-6, Garuda + Viking Icebreaker, Black Market 5/2,000. Chest IV from Lv13-15 tiles, bosses Lv7-8, Royal Thief, Viking Berserker, Black Market 5/2,500. Chest V from monsters Lv24-40, bosses Lv9-17, Lava Turtle/Golem/Witch/Warlord/Ymir Lv1-5, Hydra Lv1-4, Viking Chief, Nian, Royal Thief, Black Market 5/3,000. Chest VI from Royal Thief Lv1, Black Market 5/3,500. Magic Artwork Chest (best free source): 100% drop from Lv1 Junior Hydra (contains 3 chests; NOT Medium/Senior Hydra); Crazy Eggs give 40+/day. For a bot, farm Lv1 Junior Hydra and Crazy Eggs to fill the Art Hall (which then pays gems, stamina, speedups and teleporters).

title: Battlefield Shop Item List
summary: Battlefield Shop spends Badges or Dragon Crystals on generals, scrolls, gems, gold, resources, EXP and materials; buying gems with badges (5,000 gems = 110 badges) is an efficient conversion.
content: The Battlefield Shop (in front of the walls) spends Badges or Dragon Crystals (dc). Notable buys (badges / dc): Epic Historic General x1 = 2,040 / 6,000. Scrolls: King's Scroll Chest 360 / 1,140; Dragon Scroll Chest 1,160 / 3,420. Gems: 500 = 10/30, 1,000 = 20/60, 2,000 = 40/130, 5,000 = 110/310 (efficient badge->gem conversion). Gold: 200K = 20/50, 1M = 90/250, 1M x10 = 850/2,500. Resources: 2M = 30/90, 5M = 60/190, 20M = 260/750, 50M = 640/1,880. Monarch EXP 20,000 x20 = 680/2,000; General EXP 20,000 x20 = 340/1,000. Materials: any Lv5 = 40/130; any Lv6 = 130/380. Special Items (Gems, not badges): Battlefield Special Attack/Defense/HP Increase 9,000 gems each; Battlefield Special March Size Increase 25,000 gems. Separate Huns Invasion battlefield uses Barbarian Flags (Historic General Fragments 320/160 flags, Dragon Scroll Chest 5,500, Lv4 Skill Book Chest 3,000, Super Resource Chest 480, 100 Stamina 40, 24h Speed Up 180). For a bot, spend battlefield badges/crystals on generals and scrolls, or convert to gems/gold/resources when those are the bottleneck.

title: Consuming Return Event
summary: The single most important economy event: it REBATES a large share of resources/speedups/items you spend, so hoard resources and time big construction/training pushes to it -- this is how F2P reaches Keep 35+.
content: A recurring event that REBATES a large share of resources/speedups/items you spend during its window, so the bot should TIME big spends to it. The resource consuming return is the most important: spending resources returns huge speedups (and some resources), determining whether you reach Keep 35+ without paying (e.g. consuming 2.5B of each resource returns 640+ days of speedups plus ~500M of each). FREE-to-participate (you spend items you already have). Core strategy is a cycle: START construction upgrades during a RESOURCE return event (burn resources -> get speedups), then WAIT and COMPLETE builds during a SPEED-UP return event (burn speedups -> get resources). To set up, hoard resources for half a month+: do NOT create troops/traps/construction before the event (only minimum Lv1 troops/traps for dailies). Rough Keep level able to hit each resource tier free: 125M~K25, 500M~K27, 1.25B~K29, 2.5B~K31, 5B~K33, 10B~K35 (Jan 2025 added 35B resource / 12.5M gem tiers). During SPEED-UP return events, don't overspend resources (resource-return rewards are better); spend surplus speedups on research or subordinate-city builds. Types & consumption targets: Resource (Gems, Food, Lumber, Stone, Ore); Speed-up (general/construction/healing speedups, Gems); Speed-up trap&research (Gold, trap, research speedups); Tactical Scroll; Refining Stone; Blood of Ares; Dragon feed (returns Excellent Dragon Coins); Lv7 Material (Crafting Master). Two rotations run simultaneously (11d/3d cadence). For a bot: the master rule of economy timing -- hoard, then spend during the matching return window.

title: Hecate’s Moon Event
summary: Solo daily monster-hunt challenge scored by defeating a configurable Trial Temple boss plus monsters and Hecates; 2 free keys/day; torches expire after the event so spend them.
content: A solo monster-hunt challenge (Event Center > Hecate's Moon). Each challenge costs one Underworld Key; you get 2 keys/day free by reaching 80 or 110 daily Activity score (or buy a $9.99 package). One round: monsters spawn within ~30km one at a time (solo only, no rallying), each staying 5 minutes; defeating them earns event points and Radiant Night Torch tokens. A fixed total of 19 monsters/Hecates appear before the final boss. Visiting Hecate's true form (up to 3 times: Black Moon = boss difficulty/troop count, New Moon = boss troop type, Underworld Moon = boss buff strength) configures the final Trial Temple boss. Scoring: (1) defeat the strongest Trial Temple you can, (2) kill high-point monsters, (3) visit all 3 Hecates (2 and 3 are luck; you control 1). Point list: regular monsters 20, tougher (Kamaitachi/Behemoth/Jormungandr/Typhon/Swift Goblin) 40, each Hecate 40, Trial Temple 280-390. High-score tips: choose hardest difficulty + highest buff; with strong ground/mounted pick a SIEGE-type temple (never attack with ranged -- weak to siege). Torches redeem at the New Moon Shop and DISAPPEAR after the event -- spend promptly. General Hecate (just owned) adds a bonus torch. Ranking is by highest single-round total. Free daily play. For a bot, this is a monster-hunt event: play the 2 free keys daily, pick the hardest survivable temple, and spend torches before the event ends.

title: Revelation of Horus Event
summary: Daily quest race scored by SPEED of completing the day's (pre-revealed) quest; the quests are ordinary bot actions, so pre-stage items and fire at reset. Coins redeem for rare generals and don't expire.
content: A daily quest race. During the event ONE quest is presented each day; its title is shown in advance but details are hidden -- however you can complete it during the hidden stage by acting on the pre-revealed title. Scoring/ranking is SPEED-based: the sooner you complete the day's quest after it starts (~server reset, often ~1 hour after per DST), the higher your daily-ranking placement. Each quest has its own leaderboard. Ranking gives Horus Gold Coins (redeemed in the event shop for rare items: Meteoric Stone, Epic Historic General (Premium), Narses); coins do NOT expire. FREE-to-participate (paid packages just add coins). The quests are ordinary bot actions: Cultivate Generals (10/20/100 tiers), Refine Equipment (15/25/40), Increase troop power (50,000/100,000/150,000), Tax resources (10/15/20 times), Levy Gold (15/20/25), Offer at Shrine (3/5/10), Help Alliance members (10/20/30). Because you know the day's requirement ahead of time, the winning play is to have runestones/tributes/gold/gathered power ready and complete instantly at reset. Note the per-quest countdown -- once it ends the quest can't be completed. For a bot, time base_dev/training/daily-collect actions to land exactly at the daily quest start (pre-stage tributes for shrine offers, gold for levy, generals to cultivate).

title: Revelation of Maya Event
summary: Solo 20-stage roguelike combat event scored by kills, totems and fog-clearing; NOT tied to normal base actions -- a pure combat mini-game requiring buff-stacking and healing items.
content: A solo 20-stage roguelike combat event (not tied to normal base actions). You pick generals at Stage 1, advance stage-by-stage fighting Mayan Warriors/Guards/High Priests, occupy totem towers, dispel fog and collect buffs/runes/gold; a final boss is at stage 20. Score sources: kill enemies (Warrior 10, Guard 20, High Priest 40), dispel green fog (5 each), occupy Crescent Totem (20), Typhoon Totem (50), Poisonwood Totem (100); Holy Wrath Rune on Poisonwood gives NO score. High-score loop (author hit 11,190): defeat ALL enemies, occupy ALL towers + as many Poisonwood as possible, dispel ALL fog, and beat the stage-20 boss with maximum troops remaining (nearly 1,000 points of variance) -- requires heavy buff-stacking and healing items, expect ~10 retries from stage 1. Buff strategy: stack buffs scaling with Mayan Gold owned (Ring of the Rhine, Hand of Famine) plus Gold in Fog and Moneybags to snowball gold; run a siege-heavy formation with Unstoppable Force for unwounded wins; avoid Desperate Measures and Hand of Death (block healing); save Blade Rune and Prediction Rune for the last boss. Reset points: stages 1, 3, 8, 14, 18. Buildings: Portal (advance), Mayan Camp (recruit), Life Stream (heal), Calendar Stone (random buff), Mayan Market (buy with gold). For a bot, this is a self-contained combat puzzle -- low priority for an economy-first bot since no base action helps and it needs manual buff decisions.

title: Cleopatra’s Treasure
summary: Gacha event: farm key fragments free by hunting monsters (80/day) and gathering (20/day); repaired keys open gem-cost chests for a chance at a golden general -- but the F2P play is to DISASSEMBLE fragments for resources.
content: A gacha event mixing free grinding and paid gem spending. You collect key fragments (upper/middle/lower) and repair tools by hunting monsters (up to 80/day) and gathering resources (up to 20/day) -- ~100 fragments/day cap, FREE. Repaired keys open Cleopatra's Treasure Chest: each open costs Gems and is capped at 150/day. The chest is a gamble -- high-chance small sub-chests (resources, EXP, medals, runestones, stamina), low-chance jackpots 5K Gems, 2,500 Runestone Chest, or the highlight Epic/Golden Historic General (Premium, not at tavern). Gem cost per open rises every 10 opens (1-10 = 1,600 each up to 91-150 = 9,500; totals 16,000 for 10x, 139,000 for 50x, 970,000 for 150x). NOT reliably F2P for the general -- the author spent 120,000 gems without winning (luck-based, effectively a coining ranking). Ranking rewards come from a score accrued by collecting fragments and repairing keys. F2P value path: leftover fragments/keys auto-disappear after the event, so DISASSEMBLE them -- each fragment/tool = 2 Super Resources Chests (Gold); a repaired key gives only 2 keys, so don't repair if disassembling. At ~100/day free over 10 days = ~1,000 fragments = ~2,000 Super Resource Chests ~= ~120M of each resource. For a bot: grind fragments via monster-hunt/gather (both normal actions), then disassemble for resources rather than paying to open.

title: Dwarf’s Lucky Apple
summary: Fully F2P wish event: earn Wishing Coins free by killing monsters and gathering, spend on apple tiers for rewards (stamina, teleporters, speedups); deliver any Pie to a Witch for a big Witch's Gift.
content: A FREE gacha/wish event. You randomly earn Wishing Coins by defeating monsters or gathering resources -- both normal bot actions, so time monster-hunting and gathering to the window. Spend coins to make a wish at three apple tiers: Wealth (1 coin), Joy (3 coins), Blessing (5 coins). Each wish gives at least one item -- Wealth: ~1M resource, EXP, or a Dwarf's Delicious Pie; Joy: speedups (5h-10d), 500K Gold, Monarch Gear Lv1 chest, or a Pie; Blessing: 100 Stamina, Adv City Teleporter x10, Adv Troop March Speedup x10, Large Material Chest, Refining Stone x50, Research Stone x10, or a Pie. Key mechanic: if a wish yields a Dwarf's Delicious Pie, deliver it to a Witch on the world map for a much bigger Witch's Gift (Refining Stone x120, 90h speedups, Runestone x100, Large Material x30, Dragon Source Fragment x40, Super Resource Chest x5, Gem 15,000, or Blood of Ares x3). Every tier has a uniform Pie chance, so the coin-efficient play for maximizing Witch's Gifts is the cheapest 1-coin Wealth apple (more wishes = more Pie chances). Fully F2P-viable. For a bot: farm coins via monster kills and gathering, spend on Wealth apples, and hand every Pie to a Witch for the big gift.

title: Ekaterina’s Garden
summary: SPEND/PAY event mechanically identical to King's Party but cheaper; buying Basic Gems fills a garden for Civilization scrolls and an Epic general; not F2P-viable -- a spend-timing decision.
content: A SPEND/PAY event (~7-10 days), mechanically the same as King's Party but CHEAPER. Buying gems adds experience to a garden; each level-up gives rewards. Only BASIC (directly purchased) Gems count -- bonus gems inside packages do NOT. Cumulative Basic Gems per level (max raised from 6 to 10 in Jan 2023): Lv2 4,000 (~$20), Lv3 15,000, Lv4 25,000 (~$100), Lv5 45,000, Lv6 70,000, Lv7 100,000, Lv8 130,000, Lv9 190,000, Lv10 270,000 (~$1,100). Uniquely offers Civilization Equipment scrolls: a Conquest Civilization Scroll Chest at Lv5, and at Lv10 a Supremacy Civilization Scroll Chest plus an Epic Historic General (Premium). Other rewards: Material Bags x370, Runestone Chests x380, Super Resource Chests x135, Refining Stone x2,530, plus speedups. Best-value note vs King's Party: a Supremacy chest for almost half the price. Not F2P-viable -- value comes only from real-money gem purchases; no gathering/training/building adds garden experience. Access: castle > Super Value Event; held rarely now. For a bot: a spend-timing decision, not an action-scoring event -- ignore for mostly-F2P play, but if buying gems anyway, time purchases to this over King's Party for cheaper Civilization scrolls.

title: Chest Contents (Event chest)
summary: Reference list of what's inside various EVENT chests (Fire Spirit, Flower, Luxury Treasure, King's, Taxing Gift, Candy, Witch's Bag) -- drop-content data for valuing event rewards, not a scoring event.
content: A reference list of what's inside various EVENT chests (not a scoring event) -- useful to value drops received during events. Fire Spirit Chest (visiting a Fire Spirit during server war): one of 15/30-min or 1h Healing speedup, or 5K Monarch EXP. Flower Rewards (top performers at Arch of Triumph): one of 50 Stamina, 100K Food Box, 60-min Speed Up, or Blue Material. Luxury Treasure Chest (from Gather Troops event, quantity proportional to quests completed, max 69): gives ALL of Skill Book Page x1, Dragon Crystal x2, Refining Stone x15. King's Chest (from King's Path, quantity proportional to quests): ALL of Blue Material Chest x1, 1K General EXP x2, 10 Stamina x1. Taxing Gift (from taxing resources in the Market during event time): ALL of 50K F/L/S/O Box x2 each, Blue Material Chest x2, 10-min Speed Up x5. Candy (Login Rewards or defeating Pumpkin Monster) tiers Lv1-Lv6 each give ONE of a list scaling from 10K resource boxes/30-min speedup (Lv1) up to 1M resource boxes/500K Gold/8h speedup/Lv4 materials/5K Monarch EXP (Lv6), with higher candies also giving City Name Change, City Teleporter, 24h Speed Up, or Witch's Treasure Bags. Also lists Witch's Treasure Bag (Lv1-5), Witch's Gift, Celebration Gift and Barbarian King's Token. For a bot, this is descriptive drop-content data for valuing chests -- no player action scores points.

title: Event Package 1-5 Contents & Comparison
summary: PAID reference: five event-package price tiers ($4.99-$99.99); the $4.99 pack is the best value and buyable once/day per tier ($99.99 up to 12x/day); an Optional Chest lets you pick resources/materials/scrolls.
content: A PAID reference: event packages sold during ~11-day events in five price tiers -- $4.99 (1st), $9.99 (2nd), $19.99 (3rd), $49.99 (4th), $99.99 (5th). You can pick any tier directly now. Purchase limit: one/day per tier, EXCEPT the $99.99 pack up to 12/day. The $4.99 pack is widely considered the best value. Contents scale with tier; $4.99->$99.99 includes VIP Points (1,000->25,000), Gems (30,000->250,000), Gold (5M->55M), Speed Up (256h->5,820h), an Optional Chest (Lv1->Lv5), Blood of Ares (4->60), Covenant Stone (1->20), Subordinate City Clue Chest (20->250), Premium Gear Chest, Merit Jade, Glory Golden Twig, Forge Master Certificate, Source of Life (72->700), Adv Troop March Speedup, Dragon Gemstone, and Monarch/General EXP (50,000->1,500,000). The $99.99 tier exclusively adds an Epic Historic General (Glory), Hephaestus Hammer x5, Senior March Size Increase, Lv6 Barbarian King's Token. The Optional Chest lets you choose items by group (A resources up to 2.8B each at Lv5; F research stone/tactic scroll/research speedup; G dragon & Achaemenidae scroll chests only at Lv5; H badge/dragon crystal only at Lv5). Paying also grants a free Limited Time Promotion package sized to Basic Gems purchased. For a bot's spend logic, the $4.99 first pack is the value pick; a mostly-F2P bot skips these but should understand the Optional Chest for players who do buy.

title: Event Package – Should you buy “5th” or “a lot of 1st”?
summary: PAID value comparison: for the same ~$185, one full 1st-5th set gives the exclusive general/scrolls and more speedups; spamming the $4.99 pack maximizes gems, stamina, refining stones and promo bonuses.
content: A PAID value comparison: for the same $185, buy one full set of packs 1st->5th once, versus the $4.99 first pack 37 times. Verdict: buy the 5TH (full set) if you want the exclusive Golden/Epic Historic General, Dragon Scroll and Achaemenidae Scroll, or if you're in a hurry (37 first-packs take over a month at one/day). Buy MANY 1ST packs for maximum Gems, Stamina and Refining Stones, and to maximize the free Limited Time Promotion bonuses. At $185, 5th set vs 37x1st: 5th wins on VIP Points (45,500 vs 37,000), Resources (934M vs 869.5M), Speed Up (344d vs 159d), Gold, Lv5 Material, Research Stone, Tactic Scroll, Soul Crystal, Monarch/General EXP (2.19M vs 1.48M), and gives the exclusive general, Badge x100, Dragon Crystal x100, Dragon Scroll x2, Achaemenidae Scroll x2. 37x1st wins on Gems (962,000 vs 545,500), Refining Stone (11,100 vs 6,900), Runestone, Stamina (259 vs 102), Adv Troop March Speedup (296 vs 173), Adv Royal Waybill (37 vs 10), Chips (37,000 vs 5,000). The bundled Limited Time Promotion strongly favors many 1st packs (~592 days speedup vs ~105). Caveat: double-down coupons make buying all 5 at once give more of nearly everything. For a bot: 5th for the exclusive general/scrolls or speed; spam 1st for gems/stamina/refining stones and promo bonuses -- but this is paid-only, so a mostly-F2P bot treats it as informational.
